Show output payload 
[
  {
    "text": "Now I have a comprehensive view of all 8 files plus the style reference. Here is my complete review.\n\n---\n\n## Documentation Review Report\n\n### 1. Cross-Reference Integrity\n\n**All cross-references are valid.** Every `references/...` link in the reviewed files points to a file that actually exists on disk. The Resources section of SKILL.md (lines 100-118) lists all 9 reference files, all template directories, and all scripts -- all confirmed present. Internal cross-references between the new reference files (e.g., `transcript-archive.md` line 87 pointing to `references/global-configuration.md`, and `global-configuration.md` line 18 pointing back to `references/transcript-archive.md`) are bidirectional and correct.\n\nNo broken links found.\n\n### 2. Single Source of Truth\n\n**MEDIUM -- Auto Push retry policy defined only in git-policy.md, but the alerts.json schema in global-configuration.md defines a `push_failed` alert type without mentioning the retry-once behavior.** `git-policy.md` line 50 says \"retry once. On second failure, write an alert.\" The `push_failed` alert type in `global-configuration.md` line 63 simply says \"Push attempted but failed.\" This is acceptable -- the alert schema should not re-explain the retry policy -- but a brief parenthetical like \"(after retry)\" in the `push_failed` trigger description would improve clarity.\n\n**PASS -- config.toml schema.** Fully defined only in `global-configuration.md` (lines 33-39). README.md (lines 90-94) shows a subset (3 of 5 fields) in a user-facing summary table. This is appropriate -- README is a quick-start guide, not the schema definition. No conflict.\n\n**PASS -- alerts.json format.** Fully defined only in `global-configuration.md` (lines 45-65). Other files reference it by path (`~/.memorytree/alerts.json`) without redefining the format.\n\n**PASS -- Daemon CLI.** Fully defined in `heartbeat-scheduling.md` (lines 20-28). README.md (lines 80-86) mirrors the same 5 subcommands with the same descriptions. This is tolerable duplication for a user-facing README but worth noting.\n\n**PASS -- Execution Flow.** Fully defined in `heartbeat-scheduling.md` (lines 32-37). No other file redefines it.\n\n### 3. Factual Consistency\n\n**PASS -- `auto_push` default.** `global-configuration.md` says `true` (line 38). `README.md` says `true` (line 92). `git-policy.md` describes the `auto_push = true` behavior starting at line 46. All consistent.\n\n**PASS -- `heartbeat_interval` default.** `global-configuration.md` says `\"15m\"` (line 35). `README.md` says `\"15m\"` (line 93). Consistent.\n\n**PASS -- auto_push push-skip behavior.** SKILL.md line 95 says \"the push is skipped and an alert is written.\" `git-policy.md` line 49 says \"skip the push and write an alert.\" `heartbeat-scheduling.md` line 36 says \"If `auto_push = true` and a Git remote exists, push\" (implicitly, skip if no remote). Templates (`en/agents.md` line 32, `zh-cn/agents.md` line 32) say \"pushes automatically after committing. If a push fails, an alert is written.\" All consistent, though the template wording (\"push fails\") is slightly different from SKILL.md (\"push is skipped\") -- see finding below.\n\n**HIGH -- Inconsistent wording between templates and SKILL.md on push failure vs. push skip.** The `en/agents.md` template (line 32) says: \"If a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\" SKILL.md line 95 says: \"If no Git remote is configured, the push is skipped and an alert is written.\" These describe two different situations (push failure vs. no remote configured). The template only covers the failure case, while SKILL.md only covers the no-remote case. Both cases exist in `git-policy.md` (lines 49 and 50), but the template and SKILL.md each omit the other scenario. This is a factual gap rather than a contradiction, but it creates an incomplete picture in each location.\n\n### 4. Contract Language (heartbeat.py and memorytree-daemon not yet implemented)\n\n**CRITICAL -- `heartbeat-scheduling.md` uses present-tense language that implies the tools already exist.** Specific problematic lines:\n\n- Line 11: \"`heartbeat.py` **executes** once and exits.\"\n- Line 12: \"The OS-native scheduler **invokes** `heartbeat.py` on a fixed interval.\"\n- Line 13: \"One computer **runs** exactly one scheduled heartbeat task.\"\n- Line 16: \"The `memorytree-daemon watch` mode (continuous loop) **exists** only for development and debugging.\"\n- Line 20: \"The `memorytree-daemon` command **manages** the heartbeat lifecycle.\"\n\nThis file is the authoritative reference for the heartbeat system and contains zero indication anywhere in the document that these are contract specifications for code that does not yet exist. Compare with SKILL.md lines 112-113 which explicitly say \"(contract -- not yet implemented)\" and README.md line 96 which has a prominent Note block. The reference file itself has no such disclaimer.\n\n**CRITICAL -- `global-configuration.md` also uses present-tense with no \"not yet implemented\" disclaimer.** The entire file describes `config.toml`, `alerts.json`, `heartbeat.lock`, and the `~/.memorytree/` directory layout as if they already exist. Since the heartbeat system that creates and uses these files does not exist yet, a reader of this reference file alone would assume the infrastructure is operational.\n\n**HIGH -- `en/agents.md` and `zh-cn/agents.md` templates use present-tense for heartbeat behavior with no disclaimer.** Lines 34-38 (en) and 34-38 (zh-cn) say things like \"Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push **are handled** by the background heartbeat process\" and \"If `memorytree-daemon` **is not registered** on this machine, suggest the user run `memorytree-daemon install`.\" These templates will be placed into user repositories as `AGENTS.md`. A user reading their own `AGENTS.md` would assume `memorytree-daemon install` is a real command they can run. The README disclaimer on line 96 does not propagate into the generated AGENTS.md files.\n\n**MEDIUM -- SKILL.md \"On Activation\" step 5 (line 18) says to suggest the user run `memorytree-daemon install`.** This instructs the model to suggest running a command that does not exist. While SKILL.md does mark both items as \"(contract -- not yet implemented)\" in the Resources section (lines 112-113), the On Activation section reads as an executable instruction to the model with no guard for the case where the daemon is not yet available.\n\n**HIGH -- README.md \"Background Heartbeat\" section (lines 68-98) presents the daemon as a usable feature.** It includes an \"Install the daemon\" heading with a code block showing `memorytree-daemon install`, a CLI commands table, and a settings table. The disclaimer on line 96 (\"Note: `heartbeat.py` and `memorytree-daemon` are contract specifications -- code is not yet implemented\") appears only after all of this. A reader scanning the README would likely try the commands before reaching the note. The disclaimer should come before or at the very beginning of the \"Background Heartbeat\" section, not after.\n\n### 5. Numbering\n\n**PASS.** All numbered lists in SKILL.md are sequential with no gaps or duplicates:\n- On Activation: 1-5\n- Core Behavior: 1-13\n- Read Order: 1-6\n- Detect: 1-3\n- Upgrade: 1-5\n- Initialize: 1-10\n- Maintain: 1-8\n- Git And Safety: 1-10\n\nAll numbered lists in the reference files are also sequential.\n\n### 6. Style Consistency\n\n**MEDIUM -- `heartbeat-scheduling.md` uses \"Core Rules\" while `global-configuration.md` skips it.** The established pattern from `execution-environment.md` and `transcript-archive.md` is: \"Use this file when...\" preamble, then `## Goal`, then `## Core Rules` (or equivalent numbered rules), then domain sub-sections, then `## Stop And Ask`. `heartbeat-scheduling.md` follows this pattern correctly. `global-configuration.md` has the preamble, Goal, and Stop And Ask, but jumps directly into domain sections (Directory Layout, config.toml Schema, etc.) without a Core Rules section. This is a minor structural deviation.\n\n**MEDIUM -- `git-policy.md` has no \"Use this file when...\" preamble and no \"Goal\" section.** It opens with a statement (\"MemoryTree must adapt to the host repository\") instead of following the pattern. It does have \"Stop And Ask\" at the end. This is a pre-existing style choice for this file (it was not newly created), but the update added the \"Auto Push\" section which follows the file's existing style, so this is not a regression.\n\n**MEDIUM -- `transcript-archive.md` has no \"Stop And Ask\" section.** Most reference files that define behavioral rules include a \"Stop And Ask\" section. `transcript-archive.md` has stop-and-ask behavior embedded in its Core Rules (rule 6: \"Ask once per repository\") and Git Rules, but does not have a dedicated section. This was pre-existing.\n\n**LOW -- `global-configuration.md` \"Core Rules\" omission.** While it has enough domain-specific sections to be self-explanatory, adding a brief \"Core Rules\" section (e.g., \"1. Use `~/.memorytree/` as the single root for all global state. 2. Never write global state inside a project repository.\") would align with the other reference files.\n\n---\n\n## Summary by Severity\n\n### CRITICAL (2 findings -- must fix before commit)\n\n1. **`references/heartbeat-scheduling.md` -- no \"not yet implemented\" disclaimer.** The entire file uses present-tense language (\"executes\", \"invokes\", \"manages\", \"exists\") with zero indication that `heartbeat.py` and `memorytree-daemon` are unimplemented contracts. This will mislead any reader who reaches this file without first reading SKILL.md or README.md.\n\n2. **`references/global-configuration.md` -- no \"not yet implemented\" disclaimer.** Describes `~/.memorytree/config.toml`, `alerts.json`, `heartbeat.lock`, and the directory layout as if they already exist. The heartbeat infrastructure that creates and consumes these artifacts has not been built.\n\n### HIGH (3 findings -- should fix)\n\n3. **`assets/templates/en/agents.md` and `assets/templates/zh-cn/agents.md` -- present-tense heartbeat rules with no guard.** These templates become the user's `AGENTS.md`. They instruct the model to suggest `memorytree-daemon install` and describe heartbeat behavior as active. No disclaimer propagates from SKILL.md or README.md into the generated file.\n\n4. **README.md -- disclaimer placement.** The \"not yet implemented\" note on line 96 appears after the daemon install instructions, CLI table, and settings table. It should appear at the start of the \"Background Heartbeat\" section so readers see it before attempting to use the commands.\n\n5. **Incomplete coverage of push scenarios in templates vs. SKILL.md.** `en/agents.md` only mentions push failure; SKILL.md only mentions no-remote skip. Both scenarios are real (defined in `git-policy.md`). Each location gives an incomplete picture.\n\n### MEDIUM (4 findings -- nice to fix)\n\n6. **`global-configuration.md` lacks a \"Core Rules\" section**, deviating from the reference file style pattern.\n\n7. **`transcript-archive.md` lacks a dedicated \"Stop And Ask\" section.** Stop-and-ask behavior is embedded in other rules but not collected into the standard terminal section.\n\n8. **`git-policy.md` has no \"Use this file when...\" preamble or \"Goal\" section.** Pre-existing but worth noting since the file was updated.\n\n9. **SKILL.md \"On Activation\" step 5 instructs the model to suggest `memorytree-daemon install`** without guarding for the case where the command does not yet exist. A conditional (\"if the daemon script exists on disk\") would prevent the model from suggesting an unavailable command.\n\n### LOW (2 findings -- nitpick)\n\n10. **`global-configuration.md` `push_failed` alert type** could benefit from a \"(after retry)\" note in the trigger description to connect it with the retry-once policy in `git-policy.md`.\n\n11. **README.md duplicates the daemon CLI table** from `heartbeat-scheduling.md`. Acceptable for a user-facing README, but if the CLI changes, both locations must be updated.",

  },

## Review Summary

Two reviews found 2 CRITICAL, 4 HIGH, and several MEDIUM/LOW issues. Let me fix the actionable ones.

### CRITICAL
1. heartbeat-scheduling.md and global-configuration.md have no "not yet implemented" disclaimer
2. Same for global-configuration.md

### HIGH
3. Templates suggest memorytree-daemon install without guard for non-existence
4. README disclaimer appears after install instructions instead of before
5. Templates only mention push failure; SKILL.md only mentions no-remote skip — each is incomplete
6. Heartbeat interval: TODO.md says 5min, docs say 15min (deliberate revision — keeping 15m)

Fixing CRITICAL and HIGH issues now:

}

All fixes verified. Here's the final review outcome:

## Fixes Applied

| Severity | Issue | Fix |
|----------|-------|-----|
| CRITICAL | heartbeat-scheduling.md — no contract disclaimer | Added blockquote disclaimer after preamble |
| CRITICAL | global-configuration.md — no contract disclaimer | Added blockquote disclaimer after preamble |
| HIGH | README disclaimer appeared after install instructions | Moved to top of Background Heartbeat section, removed duplicate |
| HIGH | Templates only mentioned push failure, SKILL.md only mentioned no-remote skip | Both now cover "no remote OR push failure → alert" |
| HIGH | Templates/SKILL.md suggested memorytree-daemon install without guard | Changed to "if available but not registered" conditional |

## Deferred (MEDIUM/LOW, pre-existing or intentional)

| Issue | Reason |
|-------|--------|
| global-configuration.md lacks "Core Rules" section | Domain sections are self-explanatory; adding Core Rules would be stylistic padding |
| transcript-archive.md lacks dedicated "Stop And Ask" | Pre-existing; rules are embedded inline |
| git-policy.md has no preamble/Goal | Pre-existing style; not a regression |
| Heartbeat interval 15m vs TODO.md 5m | Deliberate revision in implementation docs; TODO.md is the older design document |
| On Activation has 5 steps (plan said 6) | Daemon check folded into step 5; functionally complete |

diff --git a/README.md b/README.md
index 89fc62e..a2d5f61 100644
--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -65,6 +65,38 @@ Memory/
 - **Locale-aware** — English and Simplified Chinese templates, auto-detects from repo content
 - **Zero dependencies** — all scripts use Python standard library only
 
+## Background Heartbeat
+
+> **Note**: `heartbeat.py` and `memorytree-daemon` are contract specifications — code is not yet implemented.
+
+MemoryTree includes a background heartbeat that automatically discovers, imports, cleans, commits, and pushes transcripts without consuming model tokens.
+
+### Install the daemon
+
+```bash
+memorytree-daemon install
+```
+
+### CLI commands
+
+| Command                     | Description |
+|-----------------------------|-------------|
+| `memorytree-daemon install`   | Register the heartbeat with the OS scheduler (cron / launchd / Task Scheduler). |
+| `memorytree-daemon uninstall` | Remove the scheduled heartbeat task. |
+| `memorytree-daemon run-once`  | Run a single heartbeat cycle immediately. |
+| `memorytree-daemon watch`     | Continuous loop for development and debugging only. |
+| `memorytree-daemon status`    | Show registration state and last execution result. |
+
+### Key settings (`~/.memorytree/config.toml`)
+
+| Setting              | Default  | Description |
+|----------------------|----------|-------------|
+| `auto_push`          | `true`   | Push to remote after each commit. Skips if no remote is configured. |
+| `heartbeat_interval` | `"15m"`  | Interval between heartbeat executions. |
+| `log_level`          | `"info"` | Log verbosity (`debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error`). |
+
+For details see [`references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`](references/heartbeat-scheduling.md) and [`references/global-configuration.md`](references/global-configuration.md).
+
 ## Update
 
 ```bash
diff --git a/SKILL.md b/SKILL.md
index 9a5f906..6f229bf 100644
--- a/SKILL.md
+++ b/SKILL.md
@@ -12,9 +12,10 @@ Use this skill for repo-level project memory. Do not treat it as an application
 When this skill is invoked, immediately perform these steps without waiting for further user input:
 
 1. Detect the repo state (`not-installed`, `partial`, or `installed`) using `references/project-detection.md`.
-2. If `not-installed`: run `scripts/init-memorytree.py` to scaffold the workspace, then report what was created.
-3. If `partial`: run `scripts/upgrade-memorytree.py` to add missing pieces, then report what was added.
-4. If `installed`: read the active goal, bound todo, and latest chat log. Report the current state (goal summary, todo progress, pending tasks) and ask the user what they want to work on next.
+2. Check `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`. If pending alerts exist, display them to the user before proceeding.
+3. If `not-installed`: run `scripts/init-memorytree.py` to scaffold the workspace, then report what was created.
+4. If `partial`: run `scripts/upgrade-memorytree.py` to add missing pieces, then report what was added.
+5. If `installed`: read the active goal, bound todo, and latest chat log. Report the current state (goal summary, todo progress, pending tasks) and ask the user what they want to work on next. If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered, suggest the user run `memorytree-daemon install`.
 
 Do not stop after detection to ask whether you should proceed. Act on the result immediately.
 
@@ -31,6 +32,8 @@ Do not stop after detection to ask whether you should proceed. Act on the result
 9. For partial repos, use the non-destructive upgrade path before manual maintenance. Use `references/upgrade-path.md`.
 10. When transcript archival is enabled, import transcripts with deterministic code, preserve raw transcripts as evidence in both the current repo mirror and the global archive, generate cleaned Markdown for indexing, keep cross-project backfills and ambiguous ownership cases in the global archive only, and ask once per repository whether raw transcript files may be committed or pushed. Use `references/transcript-archive.md`.
 11. When scripts are needed, prefer an existing `uv run python` environment first, then a system `python`, and only fall back to manual scaffolding or conservative manual review if no runner is available. Use `references/execution-environment.md`.
+12. Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.
+13. Global configuration is stored in `~/.memorytree/config.toml`. See `references/global-configuration.md`.
 
 ## Read Order
 
@@ -89,6 +92,8 @@ Do not stop after detection to ask whether you should proceed. Act on the result
 6. Keep raw transcript files under `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` in the repo, but exclude them from automatic staging until the user explicitly approved raw transcript uploads for that repository.
 7. If the diff mixes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript archives, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before committing or pushing.
 8. If the repo has protected branches or PR-only rules, follow them even if older MemoryTree rules say otherwise.
+9. When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no Git remote is configured, the push is skipped. If a push fails, the heartbeat retries once. In either case an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`. See `references/git-policy.md`.
+10. During transcript cleaning, the heartbeat scans for sensitive information (API keys, passwords, tokens). Matches are logged as warnings only — no automatic deletion or redaction. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.
 
 ## Resources
 
@@ -101,7 +106,11 @@ Do not stop after detection to ask whether you should proceed. Act on the result
 - `references/transcript-archive.md`: raw transcript archival, clean transcript indexing, per-client source rules, and repo upload confirmation rules.
 - `references/upgrade-path.md`: safe adoption flow for partial repos.
 - `references/execution-environment.md`: how to run MemoryTree scripts without assuming a project-local Python environment.
+- `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`: background heartbeat architecture, daemon CLI, and execution flow.
+- `references/global-configuration.md`: `~/.memorytree/` directory layout, `config.toml` schema, and `alerts.json` format.
 - `assets/templates/en/` and `assets/templates/zh-cn/`: seed files for English and Simplified Chinese initialization.
+- `scripts/heartbeat.py`: background heartbeat entry point (contract — not yet implemented).
+- `memorytree-daemon` CLI: heartbeat lifecycle manager (contract — not yet implemented).
 - `scripts/detect-memorytree-locale.py`: print the effective locale for a target repository.
 - `scripts/discover-transcripts.py`: scan supported local client stores, mirror current-project transcripts into the repo, and backfill other projects into the global archive only.
 - `scripts/import-transcripts.py`: import one transcript into the repo mirror and global archive, then generate clean Markdown deterministically by code.
diff --git a/assets/templates/en/agents.md b/assets/templates/en/agents.md
index 828565c..7c55f84 100644
--- a/assets/templates/en/agents.md
+++ b/assets/templates/en/agents.md
@@ -29,3 +29,10 @@
 - Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.
 - Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.
 - If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.
+- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.
+
+## Heartbeat Rules
+- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.
+- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.
+- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.
+- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, suggest the user run `memorytree-daemon install`.
diff --git a/assets/templates/zh-cn/agents.md b/assets/templates/zh-cn/agents.md
index 7dff3a0..e9747c2 100644
--- a/assets/templates/zh-cn/agents.md
+++ b/assets/templates/zh-cn/agents.md
@@ -29,3 +29,10 @@
 - 提交标题使用 MemoryTree 专用标识。优先使用 `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`；若仓库强制其他提交规范，则使用兼容格式，例如 `docs(memorytree): <subject>`。
 - 对 MemoryTree 专用变更使用专用分支和 PR；仅在仓库规则允许时才开启自动合并。
 - 如果 diff 混入产品代码、共享策略文件、跨项目 transcript 文件或文件归属不清，暂存或推送前先询问用户。
+- 当 `auto_push` 启用时，心跳进程在提交后自动推送。若未配置 remote 或推送失败，会将告警写入 `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`。
+
+## 心跳规则
+- Transcript 的发现、导入、清洗和推送由后台心跳进程负责，大模型不执行这些操作。
+- 大模型在交互会话中只负责写对话日志摘要、更新目标和待办。
+- 如果 `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` 中有待处理的通知，在会话开始时展示给用户。
+- 如果 `memorytree-daemon` 可用但当前机器未注册，建议用户运行 `memorytree-daemon install`。
diff --git a/references/git-policy.md b/references/git-policy.md
index a85ce2d..1be460d 100644
--- a/references/git-policy.md
+++ b/references/git-policy.md
@@ -41,6 +41,23 @@ Use this flow only for MemoryTree-only changes and only when the host repo does
 7. If transcript files are included, keep the PR scoped to one repository's transcript mirror only. Do not mix cross-project transcript archives into the same PR.
 8. Keep the global transcript archive updated in the same import operation, but never stage or push that user-level global archive as part of a repo PR.
 
+## Auto Push
+
+When `auto_push = true` in `~/.memorytree/config.toml`:
+
+1. The heartbeat process pushes the current branch after each commit.
+2. Before pushing, verify that a Git remote is configured. If no remote exists, skip the push and write an alert to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.
+3. If a push fails (network, authentication, rejected by server), retry once. On second failure, write an alert and continue to the next project.
+4. On the very first push for a newly registered project, confirm with the user that the remote and branch are correct.
+5. Do not force-push. Use only fast-forward pushes.
+
+When `auto_push = false`:
+
+1. The heartbeat commits locally but does not push.
+2. The user pushes manually or through the interactive skill session.
+
+Rationale: project memory is a user asset. Local-only storage risks data loss from hardware failure or accidental deletion. Automatic push to a remote provides a durable backup.
+
 ## Commit Guidance
 
 - Keep the title concise and specific.
@@ -94,3 +111,4 @@ Stop and ask the user before committing, pushing, or opening a PR when any of th
 5. The repository's review or CI rules conflict with the default MemoryTree-only flow.
 6. Raw transcript upload permission for the repository is unknown.
 7. The diff includes raw transcripts or cleaned transcript indexes from other projects.
+8. `auto_push` is enabled but the target branch is a protected branch that refuses direct pushes.
diff --git a/references/transcript-archive.md b/references/transcript-archive.md
index f5c9265..6f7c47c 100644
--- a/references/transcript-archive.md
+++ b/references/transcript-archive.md
@@ -84,6 +84,8 @@ Memory/06_transcripts/
 
 Do not place other projects' transcript mirrors inside the current repository.
 
+For the complete `~/.memorytree/` directory structure, see `references/global-configuration.md`.
+
 ## Client Sources
 
 ### Codex
@@ -129,9 +131,11 @@ Do not place other projects' transcript mirrors inside the current repository.
 
 When the user asks to search all memories or all chat history:
 
-1. Search the global cleaned transcript index first.
-2. Use the matching raw transcript files for exact confirmation.
-3. Then combine those results with the active repository's `Memory/` files when answering.
+1. Search `~/.memorytree/transcripts/index/search.sqlite` first for fast full-text lookup.
+2. Use `sessions.jsonl` for session-level metadata filtering (client, project, date range).
+3. Load the matching cleaned Markdown files for context. When exact wording matters, confirm against the raw transcript.
+4. Combine transcript results with the active repository's `Memory/` files when answering.
+5. If the search index does not exist, fall back to scanning the `clean/` directory directly.
 
 ## Git Rules

user #205 02:08:05

详细的说一下这个skill的功能

Expand full message

基于我在本次会话中读过的所有文件，以下是 MemoryTree Workflow Skill 的完整功能说明。

---

## MemoryTree Workflow — 功能全景

### 一句话定义

为任意代码仓库添加 持久化、Git 追踪的项目记忆层，让 AI 编程助手（Claude Code、Codex、Gemini CLI）跨会话保留上下文。

---

### 核心架构

你的代码仓库/
├── AGENTS.md                    ← AI 行为规则（读取顺序、记忆规则、Git 规则）
└── Memory/
    ├── 01_goals/                ← 版本化的项目目标
    ├── 02_todos/                ← 与目标绑定的任务列表
    ├── 03_chat_logs/            ← 只追加的会话日志
    ├── 04_knowledge/            ← (可选) 持久化知识笔记、架构决策
    ├── 05_archive/              ← (可选) 已完成/退役的目标和待办
    └── 06_transcripts/          ← (可选) AI 对话历史归档
        ├── clean/               ←   清洗后的 Markdown 索引
        ├── manifests/           ←   导入元数据
        └── raw/                 ←   原始 transcript 镜像

全局数据存放在 ~/.memorytree/（跨项目共享）：

~/.memorytree/
├── config.toml              ← 全局配置
├── alerts.json              ← 待处理通知
├── heartbeat.lock           ← 心跳单实例锁
├── logs/                    ← 心跳执行日志
└── transcripts/             ← 全局 transcript 归档
    ├── raw/                 ←   按 client/project/年/月 存放
    ├── clean/               ←   清洗后的 Markdown
    └── index/
        ├── sessions.jsonl   ←   会话元数据
        └── search.sqlite    ←   全文搜索索引

---

### 功能模块详解

#### 1. 自动检测与初始化

激活 Skill 时（运行 /memorytree-workflow），自动执行：

| 步骤 | 动作 |
|------|------|
| 检测状态 | 判断仓库是 not-installed、partial 还是 installed |
| 检查告警 | 读取 ~/.memorytree/alerts.json，有通知则展示 |
| 未安装 | 运行 init-memorytree.py 脚手架，创建 AGENTS.md + Memory/ |
| 部分安装 | 运行 upgrade-memorytree.py，补齐缺失文件，不覆盖已有策略 |
| 已安装 | 读取当前目标、待办、最新日志，汇报状态，询问下一步 |

不需要人工确认就直接行动——检测到状态后立即处理。

#### 2. 项目记忆管理（日常维护）

| 记忆类型 | 行为规则 |
|----------|----------|
| Goals（目标） | 只在用户明确确认需求/范围变化后才修改；版本化管理 |
| Todos（待办） | 与当前目标保持同步；进度变化时更新 |
| Chat Logs（对话日志） | 只追加，永不重写或删除历史记录 |
| Knowledge（知识） | 存放架构决策、产品想法、运维约束 |
| Archive（归档） | 过期内容从活跃文件移入归档，保持活跃文件精简 |

#### 3. 多客户端 Transcript 归档

支持从三个 AI 客户端导入对话历史：

| 客户端 | 数据源 |
|--------|--------|
| Codex | ~/.codex/sessions/*/rollout-.jsonl |
| Claude Code | ~/.claude/projects/<project>/*.jsonl |
| Gemini CLI | ~/.gemini/ 下的 saved chat 或 checkpoint |

关键原则：
- 原始 transcript 作为证据不可篡改保留
- 清洗后的 Markdown 由确定性代码生成（不消耗模型 token）
- 当前仓库只保留属于本项目的 transcript；其他项目的只进全局归档
- 首次导入时询问用户：是否允许把原始 transcript 提交到仓库

#### 4. 后台心跳进程（合约规范，代码未实现）

heartbeat.py + memorytree-daemon CLI 负责无人值守的自动化：

执行流程：

获取锁 → 加载 config.toml → 遍历注册项目 → 发现新 transcript
→ 导入 → 清洗 → commit → (auto_push 时) push → 释放锁 → 退出

Daemon CLI：

| 命令 | 说明 |
|------|------|
| memorytree-daemon install | 注册到 OS 调度器（cron / launchd / Task Scheduler） |
| memorytree-daemon uninstall | 移除定时任务 |
| memorytree-daemon run-once | 立即执行一次心跳 |
| memorytree-daemon watch | 连续循环（仅开发调试用） |
| memorytree-daemon status | 查看注册状态和上次执行结果 |

设计约束：
- 单次执行、无状态、幂等——安全重跑
- 一台电脑只能注册一个心跳任务
- 不会静默注册，安装前必须确认

#### 5. 敏感信息扫描

心跳清洗 transcript 时自动扫描 API key / password / token 等模式：
- 发现匹配 → 记录警告 + 写入 alerts.json
- 不自动删除或脱敏——由用户决定处理方式

#### 6. Git 安全策略

MemoryTree 的 Git 操作遵循严格的隔离原则：

| 规则 | 说明 |
|------|------|
| 文件隔离 | 只暂存/提交 Memory/** 和 AGENTS.md，不混入产品代码 |
| 分支隔离 | 使用专用分支 memorytree/<scope>-<date>-<slug> |
| 提交格式 | memorytree(<scope>): <subject> 或仓库兼容格式 |
| PR 隔离 | 单独 PR，不混入产品变更 |
| Auto-merge | 仅在仓库保留 review + CI 检查时才启用 |
| Auto-push | 默认开启；无 remote 则跳过并告警；只做 fast-forward |
| 受保护分支 | 绝不覆盖仓库的分支保护和 CI 规则 |

Stop And Ask（8 种场景会暂停询问用户）：混入产品代码、AGENTS.md 有冲突、目标分支不明、仓库禁止自动 PR、CI 冲突、raw transcript 权限未知、跨项目 transcript、受保护分支拒绝直推。

#### 7. 国际化

| 功能 | 说明 |
|------|------|
| 模板语言 | 英文 (en) 和简体中文 (zh-cn) 两套完整模板 |
| 自动检测 | 优先从仓库内容检测语言，其次系统语言 |
| 回复语言 | 跟随用户语言 > 仓库 locale > 系统 locale |
| 不强制翻译 | 不会为了翻译而重写已有文件 |

#### 8. 跨项目搜索

当用户要搜索所有记忆/对话历史时：

1. 先查 search.sqlite 全文索引
2. 用 sessions.jsonl 做会话级元数据过滤
3. 加载匹配的 clean Markdown；精确措辞查 raw
4. 结合当前仓库 Memory/ 文件综合回答
5. 索引不存在时降级扫描 clean/ 目录

#### 9. 执行环境适配

MemoryTree 不假设目标仓库有 Python 环境：

优先 uv run python → 退而求其次 system python → 都没有则从模板直接创建文件

永远不会自动在项目里创建 .venv 或 pyproject.toml。

---

### 设计哲学

| 原则 | 体现 |
|------|------|
| 零侵入 | 不改变仓库的分支模型、CI、review 流程 |
| 零依赖 | 所有脚本只用 Python 标准库 |
| 确定性 | transcript 清洗由代码完成，不消耗模型 token |
| 引用不重复 | 每个概念只在一个 reference 文件中定义，其他文件交叉引用 |
| 合约先行 | 先写文档规范（heartbeat、daemon），再实现代码 |
| 用户资产 | 记忆属于用户，auto_push 默认开启防止本地丢失 |

user #207 02:30:48

把这个功能说明写入 README.md

Show output payload 
1→# MemoryTree Workflow
     2→
     3→A Claude Code skill that adds persistent, Git-tracked project memory to any code repository.
     4→
     5→## What It Does
     6→
     7→MemoryTree gives AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI) a structured memory layer:
     8→
     9→- **Goals** — track your project's north star across sessions
    10→- **Todos** — version-bound task lists that stay in sync with goals
    11→- **Chat Logs** — append-only session records for full traceability
    12→- **Knowledge** — durable notes, specs, and architecture decisions
    13→- **Transcripts** — import and archive local AI chat history from Codex, Claude Code, and Gemini CLI
    14→
    15→All stored as plain Markdown files under `Memory/`, tracked by Git, no extra dependencies.
    16→
    17→## Install
    18→
    19→```bash
    20→git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow ~/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow
    21→```
    22→
    23→On Windows (Git Bash):
    24→```bash
    25→git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow "$USERPROFILE/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow"
    26→```
    27→
    28→## Usage
    29→
    30→In any Claude Code session, run:
    31→
    32→```
    33→/memorytree-workflow
    34→```
    35→
    36→The skill will immediately:
    37→1. **Detect** — check if the current repo has MemoryTree installed
    38→2. **Init** — if missing, scaffold `AGENTS.md` and `Memory/` folders
    39→3. **Upgrade** — if partial, add missing pieces without overwriting existing policy
    40→4. **Maintain** — if installed, read the active goal/todo and report current state
    41→
    42→## Project Structure
    43→
    44→After initialization, your repo will have:
    45→
    46→```
    47→AGENTS.md                    # AI behavior rules for this repo
    48→Memory/
    49→  01_goals/                  # Versioned project goals
    50→  02_todos/                  # Task lists bound to goals
    51→  03_chat_logs/              # Append-only session logs
    52→  04_knowledge/              # (optional) Durable project notes
    53→  05_archive/                # (optional) Retired goals/todos
    54→  06_transcripts/            # (optional) AI chat history archive
    55→    clean/                   #   Cleaned Markdown indexes
    56→    manifests/               #   Import metadata
    57→    raw/                     #   Raw transcript mirrors
    58→```
    59→
    60→## Features
    61→
    62→- **Multi-client transcript import** — Codex, Claude Code, Gemini CLI
    63→- **Deterministic cleaning** — raw transcripts preserved as source of truth, cleaned Markdown generated by code (not model tokens)
    64→- **Git-safe** — respects your repo's branch model, never overrides protected branches or CI checks
    65→- **Locale-aware** — English and Simplified Chinese templates, auto-detects from repo content
    66→- **Zero dependencies** — all scripts use Python standard library only
    67→
    68→## Background Heartbeat
    69→
    70→> **Note**: `heartbeat.py` and `memorytree-daemon` are contract specifications — code is not yet implemented.
    71→
    72→MemoryTree includes a background heartbeat that automatically discovers, imports, cleans, commits, and pushes transcripts without consuming model tokens.
    73→
    74→### Install the daemon
    75→
    76→```bash
    77→memorytree-daemon install
    78→```
    79→
    80→### CLI commands
    81→
    82→| Command                     | Description |
    83→|-----------------------------|-------------|
    84→| `memorytree-daemon install`   | Register the heartbeat with the OS scheduler (cron / launchd / Task Scheduler). |
    85→| `memorytree-daemon uninstall` | Remove the scheduled heartbeat task. |
    86→| `memorytree-daemon run-once`  | Run a single heartbeat cycle immediately. |
    87→| `memorytree-daemon watch`     | Continuous loop for development and debugging only. |
    88→| `memorytree-daemon status`    | Show registration state and last execution result. |
    89→
    90→### Key settings (`~/.memorytree/config.toml`)
    91→
    92→| Setting              | Default  | Description |
    93→|----------------------|----------|-------------|
    94→| `auto_push`          | `true`   | Push to remote after each commit. Skips if no remote is configured. |
    95→| `heartbeat_interval` | `"15m"`  | Interval between heartbeat executions. |
    96→| `log_level`          | `"info"` | Log verbosity (`debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error`). |
    97→
    98→For details see [`references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`](references/heartbeat-scheduling.md) and [`references/global-configuration.md`](references/global-configuration.md).
    99→
   100→## Update
   101→
   102→```bash
   103→cd ~/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow && git pull
   104→```
   105→
   106→## Requirements
   107→
   108→- [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/claude-code) CLI
   109→- Python 3.9+ (for transcript scripts; prefer `uv run python`)
   110→- Git
   111→
   112→## License
   113→
   114→MIT
   115→

Write input={"content":"# MemoryTree Workflow\n\nA Claude Code skill that adds persistent, Git-tracked project memory to any code repository.\n\n## What It Does\n\nMemoryTree gives AI codin...
Show input payload 
{
  "file_path": "D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow\\README.md",
  "content": "# MemoryTree Workflow\n\nA Claude Code skill that adds persistent, Git-tracked project memory to any code repository.\n\n## What It Does\n\nMemoryTree gives AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI) a structured memory layer that survives across sessions:\n\n- **Goals** — track your project's north star across sessions\n- **Todos** — version-bound task lists that stay in sync with goals\n- **Chat Logs** — append-only session records for full traceability\n- **Knowledge** — durable notes, specs, and architecture decisions\n- **Transcripts** — import and archive local AI chat history from Codex, Claude Code, and Gemini CLI\n\nAll stored as plain Markdown files under `Memory/`, tracked by Git, no extra dependencies.\n\n## Install\n\n```bash\ngit clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow ~/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow\n```\n\nOn Windows (Git Bash):\n```bash\ngit clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow \"$USERPROFILE/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow\"\n```\n\n## Usage\n\nIn any Claude Code session, run:\n\n```\n/memorytree-workflow\n```\n\nThe skill will immediately:\n1. **Detect** — check if the current repo has MemoryTree installed\n2. **Alert** — display pending notifications from `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`\n3. **Init** — if missing, scaffold `AGENTS.md` and `Memory/` folders\n4. **Upgrade** — if partial, add missing pieces without overwriting existing policy\n5. **Maintain** — if installed, read the active goal/todo and report current state\n\nNo manual confirmation needed — the skill acts on the detection result immediately.\n\n## Architecture\n\n### Per-Repository Structure\n\nAfter initialization, your repo will have:\n\n```\nAGENTS.md                    # AI behavior rules for this repo\nMemory/\n  01_goals/                  # Versioned project goals\n  02_todos/                  # Task lists bound to goals\n  03_chat_logs/              # Append-only session logs\n  04_knowledge/              # (optional) Durable project notes\n  05_archive/                # (optional) Retired goals/todos\n  06_transcripts/            # (optional) AI chat history archive\n    clean/                   #   Cleaned Markdown indexes\n    manifests/               #   Import metadata\n    raw/                     #   Raw transcript mirrors\n```\n\n### Global Directory (`~/.memorytree/`)\n\nCross-project shared state:\n\n```\n~/.memorytree/\n  config.toml                # User-level settings\n  alerts.json                # Pending notifications\n  heartbeat.lock             # Single-instance lock\n  logs/                      # Heartbeat execution logs\n  transcripts/               # Global transcript archive\n    raw/                     #   By client/project/year/month\n    clean/                   #   Cleaned Markdown\n    index/\n      sessions.jsonl         #   Session metadata\n      search.sqlite          #   Full-text search index\n```\n\nFor the full schema see [`references/global-configuration.md`](references/global-configuration.md).\n\n## Features\n\n### Memory Management\n\n| Memory Type | Behavior |\n|-------------|----------|\n| **Goals** | Changed only after explicit user confirmation of a scope or requirement change. Versioned. |\n| **Todos** | Kept in sync with the active goal. Updated when progress changes or next task becomes clearer. |\n| **Chat Logs** | Append-only. Never rewritten or deleted. |\n| **Knowledge** | Stores architecture decisions, product ideas, operating constraints. |\n| **Archive** | Stale context moved here to keep active files concise. |\n\n### Multi-Client Transcript Import\n\nImport AI chat history from three clients:\n\n| Client | Source |\n|--------|--------|\n| **Codex** | `~/.codex/sessions/**/rollout-*.jsonl` |\n| **Claude Code** | `~/.claude/projects/<project>/*.jsonl` |\n| **Gemini CLI** | Saved chats or checkpoints under `~/.gemini/` |\n\nKey principles:\n\n- Raw transcripts preserved unchanged as source of truth\n- Cleaned Markdown generated by **deterministic code** (not model tokens)\n- Current repo only stores transcripts belonging to this project; others go to global archive only\n- First import asks once whether raw transcripts may be committed to the repo\n\n### Cross-Project Search\n\nWhen searching all memories or chat history:\n\n1. Query `search.sqlite` for fast full-text lookup\n2. Use `sessions.jsonl` for session-level metadata filtering (client, project, date range)\n3. Load matching cleaned Markdown for context; confirm exact wording against raw transcripts\n4. Combine with the current repo's `Memory/` files\n5. Fall back to scanning `clean/` directory if the index does not exist\n\n### Git Safety\n\nMemoryTree follows strict isolation rules to never interfere with your repo's workflow:\n\n| Rule | Description |\n|------|-------------|\n| File isolation | Only stages/commits `Memory/**` and `AGENTS.md` — never product code |\n| Branch isolation | Uses dedicated branch `memorytree/<scope>-<date>-<slug>` |\n| Commit format | `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>` or repo-compatible equivalent |\n| PR isolation | Separate PR for MemoryTree-only changes |\n| Auto-merge | Only when repo still enforces its review and CI checks |\n| Auto-push | On by default; skips if no remote; fast-forward only |\n| Protected branches | Never overrides branch protection or CI rules |\n\nThe skill stops and asks before proceeding in 8 scenarios: mixed product code, AGENTS.md conflicts, unclear target branch, repo forbids bot pushes, CI conflicts, unknown raw transcript permission, cross-project transcripts, or protected branch refusal.\n\nFor the full policy see [`references/git-policy.md`](references/git-policy.md).\n\n### Sensitive Info Scanning\n\nDuring transcript cleaning, the heartbeat scans for API keys, passwords, tokens, and other secrets:\n\n- Matches are logged as warnings and written to `alerts.json`\n- **No automatic deletion or redaction** — the user decides how to handle them\n\n### Locale Support\n\n| Feature | Description |\n|---------|-------------|\n| Templates | English (`en`) and Simplified Chinese (`zh-cn`) |\n| Auto-detect | Detects language from repo content first, then system locale |\n| Reply language | Follows user language > repo locale > system locale |\n| Non-destructive | Never rewrites existing files just to translate them |\n\n### Environment Adaptation\n\nMemoryTree does not assume the target repo has a Python environment:\n\n```\nPrefer uv run python → fall back to system python → fall back to direct template scaffolding\n```\n\nNever auto-creates `.venv` or `pyproject.toml` inside the project.\n\n## Background Heartbeat\n\n> **Note**: `heartbeat.py` and `memorytree-daemon` are contract specifications — code is not yet implemented.\n\nThe heartbeat automates transcript discovery, import, cleaning, commit, and push without consuming model tokens or requiring human intervention.\n\n**Execution flow**:\n\n```\nAcquire lock → Load config.toml → Iterate projects → Discover → Import\n→ Clean → Commit → (auto_push) Push → Release lock → Exit\n```\n\n### Install the daemon\n\n```bash\nmemorytree-daemon install\n```\n\n### CLI commands\n\n| Command                     | Description |\n|-----------------------------|-------------|\n| `memorytree-daemon install`   | Register the heartbeat with the OS scheduler (cron / launchd / Task Scheduler). |\n| `memorytree-daemon uninstall` | Remove the scheduled heartbeat task. |\n| `memorytree-daemon run-once`  | Run a single heartbeat cycle immediately. |\n| `memorytree-daemon watch`     | Continuous loop for development and debugging only. |\n| `memorytree-daemon status`    | Show registration state and last execution result. |\n\n### Key settings (`~/.memorytree/config.toml`)\n\n| Setting              | Default  | Description |\n|----------------------|----------|-------------|\n| `auto_push`          | `true`   | Push to remote after each commit. Skips if no remote is configured. |\n| `heartbeat_interval` | `\"15m\"`  | Interval between heartbeat executions. |\n| `log_level`          | `\"info\"` | Log verbosity (`debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error`). |\n\n### Design constraints\n\n- Single execution, stateless, idempotent — safe to re-run at any interval\n- One computer = one scheduled heartbeat task\n- Will not silently register — always confirms before `install`\n\nFor details see [`references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`](references/heartbeat-scheduling.md) and [`references/global-configuration.md`](references/global-configuration.md).\n\n## Design Principles\n\n| Principle | How |\n|-----------|-----|\n| **Zero intrusion** | Never changes the repo's branch model, CI, or review process |\n| **Zero dependencies** | All scripts use Python standard library only |\n| **Deterministic** | Transcript cleaning done by code, not model tokens |\n| **Single source of truth** | Each concept defined in one reference file, others cross-reference |\n| **Contract first** | Document the spec (heartbeat, daemon) before writing code |\n| **User asset** | Memory belongs to the user; auto_push on by default to prevent local data loss |\n\n## Reference Docs\n\n| File | Purpose |\n|------|---------|\n| [`references/project-detection.md`](references/project-detection.md) | Detect install state |\n| [`references/memory-layout.md`](references/memory-layout.md) | Folder and file naming rules |\n| [`references/update-rules.md`](references/update-rules.md) | When to version goals, todos, and session logs |\n| [`references/git-policy.md`](references/git-policy.md) | Git defaults, auto-push, and policy merge |\n| [`references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`](references/heartbeat-scheduling.md) | Heartbeat architecture, daemon CLI, execution flow |\n| [`references/global-configuration.md`](references/global-configuration.md) | `~/.memorytree/` layout, `config.toml` schema, `alerts.json` format |\n| [`references/transcript-archive.md`](references/transcript-archive.md) | Transcript archival, cleaning, search, and upload rules |\n| [`references/locale-selection.md`](references/locale-selection.md) | Locale choice rules |\n| [`references/response-language.md`](references/response-language.md) | Reply-language precedence |\n| [`references/execution-environment.md`](references/execution-environment.md) | How to run scripts without a project-local Python environment |\n| [`references/upgrade-path.md`](references/upgrade-path.md) | Safe adoption flow for partial repos |\n\n## Update\n\n```bash\ncd ~/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow && git pull\n```\n\n## Requirements\n\n- [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/claude-code) CLI\n- Python 3.9+ (for transcript scripts; prefer `uv run python`)\n- Git\n\n## License\n\nMIT\n"
}

Done. Commit a32a4d4 已推送到 GitHub。

README.md 现在包含完整的功能说明：
- Architecture — 仓库内结构 + 全局 ~/.memorytree/ 目录
- Memory Management — 5 种记忆类型的行为规则
- Multi-Client Transcript Import — Codex / Claude Code / Gemini CLI
- Cross-Project Search — 5 步搜索策略
- Git Safety — 7 条隔离规则 + 8 种 Stop And Ask 场景
- Sensitive Info Scanning — 扫描但不自动删除
- Locale Support — 中英双语模板 + 自动检测
- Background Heartbeat — daemon CLI + 执行流程 + 配置
- Design Principles — 6 条设计哲学
- Reference Docs — 11 个 reference 文件的完整索引

1→# MemoryTree Workflow
     2→
     3→A Claude Code skill that adds persistent, Git-tracked project memory to any code repository.
     4→
     5→## What It Does
     6→
     7→MemoryTree gives AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI) a structured memory layer that survives across sessions:
     8→
     9→- **Goals** — track your project's north star across sessions
    10→- **Todos** — version-bound task lists that stay in sync with goals
    11→- **Chat Logs** — append-only session records for full traceability
    12→- **Knowledge** — durable notes, specs, and architecture decisions
    13→- **Transcripts** — import and archive local AI chat history from Codex, Claude Code, and Gemini CLI
    14→
    15→All stored as plain Markdown files under `Memory/`, tracked by Git, no extra dependencies.
    16→
    17→## Install
    18→

    20→git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow ~/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow\\README.md",
  "old_string": "# MemoryTree Workflow\n\nA Claude Code skill that adds persistent, Git-tracked project memory to any code repository.\n\n## What It Does\n\nMemoryTree gives AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI) a structured memory layer that survives across sessions:\n\n- **Goals** — track your project's north star across sessions\n- **Todos** — version-bound task lists that stay in sync with goals\n- **Chat Logs** — append-only session records for full traceability\n- **Knowledge** — durable notes, specs, and architecture decisions\n- **Transcripts** — import and archive local AI chat history from Codex, Claude Code, and Gemini CLI\n\nAll stored as plain Markdown files under `Memory/`, tracked by Git, no extra dependencies.",
  "new_string": "<div align=\"center\">\n\n<img src=\"https://raw.githubusercontent.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/master/assets/logo.svg\" alt=\"MemoryTree\" width=\"120\" />\n\n# MemoryTree Workflow\n\n**Persistent, Git-tracked project memory for AI coding assistants.**\n\n[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE)\n[![Python 3.9+](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-3.9%2B-3776AB.svg?logo=python&logoColor=white)](https://www.python.org/)\n[![Claude Code](https://img.shields.io/badge/Claude_Code-Skill-cc785c.svg?logo=anthropic&logoColor=white)](https://claude.ai/claude-code)\n[![Platform](https://img.shields.io/badge/Platform-macOS%20%7C%20Linux%20%7C%20Windows-lightgrey.svg)]()\n\n<p>\n  <a href=\"#install\">Install</a> &nbsp;&bull;&nbsp;\n  <a href=\"#usage\">Usage</a> &nbsp;&bull;&nbsp;\n  <a href=\"#features\">Features</a> &nbsp;&bull;&nbsp;\n  <a href=\"#background-heartbeat\">Heartbeat</a> &nbsp;&bull;&nbsp;\n  <a href=\"#reference-docs\">Docs</a>\n</p>\n\n</div>\n\n---\n\n## What It Does\n\nMemoryTree gives AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI) a structured memory layer that survives across sessions:\n\n| | Feature | Description |\n|---|---------|-------------|\n| :dart: | **Goals** | Track your project's north star across sessions |\n| :white_check_mark: | **Todos** | Version-bound task lists that stay in sync with goals |\n| :speech_balloon: | **Chat Logs** | Append-only session records for full traceability |\n| :books: | **Knowledge** | Durable notes, specs, and architecture decisions |\n| :inbox_tray: | **Transcripts** | Import and archive AI chat history from Codex, Claude Code, and Gemini CLI |\n\nAll stored as plain Markdown files under `Memory/`, tracked by Git, zero dependencies."
}

     2→
     3→<img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/master/assets/logo.svg" alt="MemoryTree" width="120" />
     4→
     5→# MemoryTree Workflow
     6→
     7→**Persistent, Git-tracked project memory for AI coding assistants.**
     8→
     9→[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE)
    10→[![Python 3.9+](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-3.9%2B-3776AB.svg?logo=python&logoColor=white)](https://www.python.org/)
    11→[![Claude Code](https://img.shields.io/badge/Claude_Code-Skill-cc785c.svg?logo=anthropic&logoColor=white)](https://claude.ai/claude-code)
    12→[![Platform](https://img.shields.io/badge/Platform-macOS%20%7C%20Linux%20%7C%20Windows-lightgrey.svg)]()
    13→
    14→<p>
    15→  <a href="#install">Install</a> &nbsp;&bull;&nbsp;
    16→  <a href="#usage">Usage</a> &nbsp;&bull;&nbsp;
    17→  <a href="#features">Features</a> &nbsp;&bull;&nbsp;
    18→  <a href="#background-heartbeat">Heartbeat</a> &nbsp;&bull;&nbsp;
    19→  <a href="#reference-docs">Docs</a>
    20→</p>
    21→
    22→</div>
    23→
    24→---
    25→
    26→## What It Does
    27→
    28→MemoryTree gives AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI) a structured memory layer that survives across sessions:
    29→
    30→| | Feature | Description |
    31→|---|---------|-------------|
    32→| :dart: | **Goals** | Track your project's north star across sessions |
    33→| :white_check_mark: | **Todos** | Version-bound task lists that stay in sync with goals |
    34→| :speech_balloon: | **Chat Logs** | Append-only session records for full traceability |
    35→| :books: | **Knowledge** | Durable notes, specs, and architecture decisions |
    36→| :inbox_tray: | **Transcripts** | Import and archive AI chat history from Codex, Claude Code, and Gemini CLI |
    37→
    38→All stored as plain Markdown files under `Memory/`, tracked by Git, zero dependencies.
    39→
    40→## Install
    41→
    42→```bash
    43→git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow ~/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow
    44→```
    45→
    46→On Windows (Git Bash):
    47→```bash
    48→git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow "$USERPROFILE/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow"
    49→```
    50→

{
  "description": "Review 1: README visual & structure",
  "prompt": "You are a senior technical writer reviewing the README.md of a GitHub project at D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow.\n\nThe README just had its header beautified with a logo, badges, navigation bar, and emoji feature table. Your job is to review the ENTIRE README for **visual quality, structural consistency, and presentation issues** when rendered on GitHub.\n\n## Review checklist\n\n1. **Header HTML**: Check the centered `<div>` block for valid HTML that renders correctly on GitHub. GitHub sanitizes HTML — verify no unsupported tags or attributes are used (e.g., `<div align=\"center\">` is supported, but `<style>` is not).\n\n2. **Badge links**: Check each badge URL:\n   - Does the LICENSE badge link to a file that exists? (Check if LICENSE file exists in the repo)\n   - Do badge image URLs use valid shields.io format?\n   - Does the Claude Code badge link work?\n   - Is the Platform badge link empty `()` — is that OK or will it cause issues?\n\n3. **Navigation anchors**: Verify each `<a href=\"#...\">` in the nav bar matches an actual `##` heading in the file. Check for case sensitivity and special character issues (GitHub auto-generates anchors from headings).\n\n4. **Emoji rendering**: The feature table uses GitHub emoji shortcodes (`:dart:`, `:white_check_mark:`, etc.). Verify these are valid GitHub emoji codes that will render correctly.\n\n5. **Logo SVG**: Read `D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow\\assets\\logo.svg` and check:\n   - Is the SVG valid and renderable?\n   - Does the `<img src=\"...\">` URL in README match where the file actually is? (The URL uses `raw.githubusercontent.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/master/assets/logo.svg`)\n   - Will it display correctly on GitHub (GitHub renders SVG from raw.githubusercontent.com)?\n\n6. **Table formatting**: Check all Markdown tables in the README for consistent column alignment and proper rendering.\n\n7. **Overall flow**: Does the README read well from top to bottom? Is the information hierarchy logical? Are there any jarring transitions between sections?\n\n8. **Link integrity**: Check ALL markdown links `[text](url)` and `[text](#anchor)` in the entire file — do they point to valid targets?\n\nRead the full README.md and assets/logo.svg. Also check if a LICENSE file exists (for badge link validation).\n\nReport findings as:\n- CRITICAL: Must fix\n- HIGH: Should fix\n- MEDIUM: Nice to fix\n- LOW: Nitpick\n\nDo NOT make any edits. Research only."
}

{
  "description": "Review 2: content accuracy audit",
  "prompt": "You are a factual accuracy reviewer for the README.md of a project at D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow.\n\nThe README was recently expanded with comprehensive feature documentation. Your job is to verify that **every claim in the README accurately reflects what the actual reference docs and SKILL.md say**. No fabrication, no misrepresentation.\n\n## Review checklist\n\n1. **Usage steps**: README says the skill does 5 steps (Detect, Alert, Init, Upgrade, Maintain). Verify this matches SKILL.md \"On Activation\" section exactly — same count, same order, same behavior described.\n\n2. **Memory Management table**: README describes behavior rules for Goals, Todos, Chat Logs, Knowledge, Archive. Cross-check each against SKILL.md \"Maintain\" section and the agent templates.\n\n3. **Multi-Client Transcript Import table**: README lists specific source paths for Codex, Claude Code, Gemini CLI. Verify these paths match `references/transcript-archive.md` \"Client Sources\" section exactly.\n\n4. **Cross-Project Search**: README lists a 5-step search process. Verify it matches `references/transcript-archive.md` \"Search Rules\" exactly — same order, same steps.\n\n5. **Git Safety table**: README lists 7 rules and says \"8 scenarios\" for Stop And Ask. Verify the 7 rules against `references/git-policy.md` and verify the 8 Stop And Ask scenarios match.\n\n6. **Sensitive Info Scanning**: README says \"logged as warnings and written to alerts.json, no automatic deletion\". Verify against `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.\n\n7. **Locale Support table**: Verify claims against `references/locale-selection.md` and `references/response-language.md`.\n\n8. **Background Heartbeat section**: Verify execution flow, CLI commands (5), config settings (3 fields + defaults) against `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md` and `references/global-configuration.md`.\n\n9. **Design constraints**: README says \"one computer = one scheduled heartbeat task\". Verify against heartbeat-scheduling.md Core Rules.\n\n10. **Design Principles table**: Verify each principle is actually reflected in the reference docs (not just aspirational).\n\n11. **Reference Docs table**: Verify all 11 entries — does each file path exist? Does each \"Purpose\" accurately describe the file?\n\nRead README.md, SKILL.md, and ALL files in references/. Report findings as:\n- CRITICAL: Factual error that misleads readers\n- HIGH: Inaccuracy or mismatch\n- MEDIUM: Imprecise wording\n- LOW: Nitpick\n\nDo NOT make any edits. Research only."
}

Usage Steps (README lines 59-66 vs SKILL.md \"On Activation\" lines 12-18)\n\nREADME says:\n1. Detect -- check if the current repo has MemoryTree installed\n2. Alert -- display pending notifications from `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`\n3. Init -- if missing, scaffold `AGENTS.md` and `Memory/` folders\n4. Upgrade -- if partial, add missing pieces without overwriting existing policy\n5. Maintain -- if installed, read the active goal/todo and report current state\n\nSKILL.md says:\n1. Detect the repo state using `references/project-detection.md`\n2. Check `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`. Display pending alerts before proceeding.\n3. If `installed`: read active goal, bound todo, latest chat log. Report and ask user what to work on next. Also suggest `memorytree-daemon install` if daemon available but not registered.\n\n**Finding: LOW** -- The count (5 steps), order, and behavior match. Search `~/.memorytree/transcripts/index/search.sqlite` first for fast full-text lookup.\n2. Use `sessions.jsonl` for session-level metadata filtering (client, project, date range).\n3. Load the matching cleaned Markdown files for context. When exact wording matters, confirm against the raw transcript.\n4. Combine transcript results with the active repository's `Memory/` files when answering.\n5. If the search index does not exist, fall back to scanning the `clean/` directory directly.\n\n**Finding: OK** -- All 5 steps match in count, order, and substance. This is a negligible difference.\n\n---\n\n### 5. Diff touches product code or files outside whitelist\n2. `AGENTS.md` has user-managed policy content that cannot be safely merged\n3. Target branch, base branch, or PR destination is unclear\n4. Repo forbids bot pushes, automated PRs, or auto-merge\n5. Repo's review or CI rules conflict with default MemoryTree-only flow\n6. Raw transcript upload permission for the repository is unknown\n7. Diff includes raw transcripts or cleaned transcript indexes from other projects\n8. `auto_push` enabled but target branch is protected and refuses direct pushes\n\nREADME's inline list: \"mixed product code, AGENTS.md conflicts, unclear target branch, repo forbids bot pushes, CI conflicts, unknown raw transcript permission, cross-project transcripts, or protected branch refusal.\"\n\n**Finding: OK** -- The 8 scenarios match. README correctly summarizes the behavior.\n\n---\n\n### 7. The reply-language chain does NOT include \"system locale\" at all -- that belongs to the initialization locale chain. |\n| Non-destructive: Never rewrites existing files just to translate them | response-language.md line 33: \"Do not rewrite existing MemoryTree files only to translate them.\" | **OK** |\n\n---\n\n### 8. Acquire `~/.memorytree/heartbeat.lock`. If lock held, exit immediately.\n2. Load `~/.memorytree/config.toml`.\n3. Iterate over registered projects in `config.toml`.\n4. For each project: discover new transcripts -> import -> clean -> commit.\n5. If `auto_push = true` and a Git remote exists, push.\n6. Release the lock and exit.\n\n**Finding: OK** -- README's condensed flow matches the source accurately.\n\n**CLI commands** (README lines 212-218 lists 5 commands):\n\n| README | Source (`heartbeat-scheduling.md` lines 24-30) | Verdict |\n|---|---|---|\n| `install` | install | **OK** |\n| `uninstall` | uninstall | **OK** |\n| `run-once` | run-once | **OK** |\n| `watch` | watch | **OK** |\n| `status` | status | **OK** |\n\n**Finding: OK** -- All 5 CLI commands match.\n\n**Config settings** (README lines 222-226 lists 3 fields):\n\n| README field | Source (`global-configuration.md` lines 35-41) | Verdict |\n|---|---|---|\n| `auto_push` default `true` | `auto_push` boolean default `true` | **OK** |\n| `heartbeat_interval` default `\"15m\"` | `heartbeat_interval` string default `\"15m\"` | **OK** |\n| `log_level` default `\"info\"` | `log_level` string default `\"info\"` | **OK** |\n\n**Finding: MEDIUM** -- README says \"Key settings\" and lists 3 fields, which is accurate as far as it goes. contents.\n\n---\n\n## Summary of Findings\n\n### CRITICAL: None\n\n### HIGH (2 findings)\n\n1. **Reply-language precedence is wrong** (README line 178). README says \"Follows user language > repo locale > system locale.\" The actual precedence from `references/response-language.md` is: user explicit request > user's current message language > repository locale > active MemoryTree template locale > `en`. The README incorrectly includes \"system locale\" as a fallback (it is not part of the reply-language chain at all -- it belongs only to the initialization locale chain in `locale-selection.md`) and omits the \"template locale\" step entirely.\n\n2. **\"Zero dependencies\" principle is unsubstantiated** (README line 241). README claims \"All scripts use Python standard library only.\" No reference document makes this claim. `references/execution-environment.md` discusses how to run Python but never constrains scripts to stdlib-only. This principle may be true in practice but is not documented anywhere in the reference docs and could mislead readers if it turns out scripts need third-party libraries.\n\n### MEDIUM (5 findings)\n\n1. **Knowledge description overstates exclusivity** (README line 117). README says Knowledge \"Stores architecture decisions, product ideas, operating constraints.\" SKILL.md Maintain #5 says these items belong in \"goal or knowledge content\" -- the README omits that goals can also hold this information.\n\n2. **Archive description is incomplete** (README line 118). README says stale context is \"moved here.\" SKILL.md Maintain #4 says stale context goes to \"archive or knowledge files.\" Knowledge is also a valid destination.\n\n3. **Claude Code transcript source path** (README line 127). README lists `~/.claude/projects/<project>/*.jsonl` as the source. `references/transcript-archive.md` says the primary preference is `transcript_path` when the client exposes it; the path in the README is the secondary preference.\n\n4. **Gemini CLI transcript source path** (README line 128). README says \"Saved chats or checkpoints under `~/.gemini/`\" which is vague. The reference doc specifies `~/.gemini/tmp/<project_hash>/checkpoints` and `~/.gemini/history/<project_hash>` as specific paths. The other two rows give exact glob patterns, making this inconsistency in specificity potentially confusing.\n\n5. **File isolation rule omits caveats** (README line 153). README says \"Only stages/commits `Memory/**` and `AGENTS.md`.\" The source has two important qualifiers: `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` is excluded until the user explicitly approves, and `AGENTS.md` is only included when managed by MemoryTree or when the user approved the merge.\n\n### LOW (3 findings)\n\n1. **Step 5 (Maintain) omits follow-up behavior** (README line 64). README says \"report current state\" but SKILL.md also says \"ask the user what they want to work on next\" and suggests `memorytree-daemon install` if the daemon is available but not registered.\n\n2. **Todos summary omits \"milestones move\"** (README line 115). SKILL.md Maintain #2 lists \"progress changes, milestones move, or the next task becomes clearer\" but README only mentions two of the three triggers.\n\n3. **Cross-project search step 3 wording** (README line 141). README says \"confirm exact wording against raw transcripts\" unconditionally; source says \"When exact wording matters, confirm against the raw transcript\" (conditional). Minor semantic difference.",
    "type": "text"
  },
  {
    "type": "text",
    "text": "agentId: ad6179f7784b2d9d5 (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)\n<usage>total_tokens: 42583\ntool_uses: 14\nduration_ms: 151266</usage>"
  }
]

user #276 02:45:11

有个需求是这样的，比如用户在这个会话中讨论了很长的实际，积累了大量的聊天会话，但是最终他面临的问题没有解决。这个时候他就开了一个新的窗口，想继续前面哪个会话的问题，但是这个时候，还需要用户重新描述。所有我想我们的skill要增加一个功能就是 如果用户新开会话，说看一下我最近的一次聊天是什么内容，我们就可以马上触发心跳机制把会话都同步到我们的的memory文件夹中，然后准确的找到哪个对话是最新的是不是这个项目目录的，然后总结输出一下。请先理解一下我的需求，我们只讨论 先不修改 ultrathink

assistant #277 02:46:25

我完全理解你的需求。让我梳理一下这个场景和涉及的设计考量。 --- ## 场景还原 ``` 会话 A（长时间，问题未解决） └─ 用户讨论了很久，积累大量上下文 └─ 问题没有解决，会话结束 会话 B（新窗口，同一项目目录） └─ 用户说："看看我最近的聊天是什么内容" └─ 期望：AI 立刻知道会话 A 的完整上下文，总结输出，无缝继续 ``` **核心痛点**：新会话没有旧会话的上下文，用户被迫重新描述所有背景——费时、有损、...

Expand full message

我完全理解你的需求。让我梳理一下这个场景和涉及的设计考量。

---

## 场景还原

会话 A（长时间，问题未解决）
  └─ 用户讨论了很久，积累大量上下文
  └─ 问题没有解决，会话结束

会话 B（新窗口，同一项目目录）
  └─ 用户说："看看我最近的聊天是什么内容"
  └─ 期望：AI 立刻知道会话 A 的完整上下文，总结输出，无缝继续

核心痛点：新会话没有旧会话的上下文，用户被迫重新描述所有背景——费时、有损、体验差。

---

## 这和现有心跳机制的关系

现有的心跳是定时后台同步（每 15 分钟），但这个场景需要的是 按需即时同步——用户刚关掉会话 A，马上开了会话 B，心跳可能还没跑过，最新的 transcript 还没进 Memory。

所以这不是心跳的职责，而是一个新的交互行为：

| 机制 | 触发方式 | 时效性 | 目的 |
|------|----------|--------|------|
| 心跳 | OS 调度器定时 | 延迟 ≤ 15 分钟 | 后台归档，持久化 |
| 新功能 | 用户在会话中请求 | 即时 | 上下文恢复，继续工作 |

---

## 我理解的完整流程

用户在新会话中说"看看我最近的聊天"时，Skill 应该：

第一步：即时同步
- 立刻调用 discover-transcripts.py（或类似的即时导入路径），不等心跳
- 只扫描 Claude Code 的本地 transcript 存储（~/.claude/projects/<project>/）
- 用当前项目目录做匹配，筛选出属于本项目的 transcript

第二步：定位最近会话
- 按时间戳排序，找到最新的那个 transcript
- 排除当前会话自身（刚开始的会话 B）
- 确认这就是"会话 A"

第三步：总结输出
- 读取该 transcript 的内容
- 提取关键信息：
- 在讨论什么问题？
- 尝试了哪些方案？
- 走到了哪一步？
- 什么没有解决？最后卡在哪里？
- 以结构化的方式输出给用户

第四步：无缝继续
- 用户看完总结后，直接说"继续"就能接上

---

## 几个需要想清楚的设计问题

### 1. 即时同步的执行方式

有两个选择：

- A：Skill 直接调用 discover/import 脚本——即时、可靠，但绕过了心跳的统一入口
- B：调用 memorytree-daemon run-once——复用心跳逻辑，但会同步所有项目，可能比"只同步当前项目"慢

我倾向 A，因为用户要的是快速恢复上下文，不需要全量同步。可以设计一个轻量的"单项目即时同步"路径。

### 2. 总结是否消耗 model token

现有规则说"transcript 清洗由确定性代码完成，不消耗 token"。但这里的总结不是清洗——它是一个有智能的摘要，必须用模型来做。

这和心跳的"清洗"职责不同：
- 清洗：格式转换、去噪、生成索引 → 确定性代码
- 总结：理解对话语义、提取未解决问题 → 必须用模型

所以这个功能本质上是模型在交互会话中读取 transcript 并生成延续摘要，符合现有规则"大模型只做 chat_log 摘要"的精神。

### 3. 和现有 chat_log 的关系

Memory/03_chat_logs/ 已经有会话日志，但：
- 如果会话 A 没有正常结束（用户直接关窗口），chat_log 可能没写完
- chat_log 是会话期间写的摘要，不是事后的全面复盘
- transcript 才是完整的原始记录

所以这个功能的数据源应该是 transcript（优先）+ chat_log（补充）。

### 4. "最近的聊天"的范围

用户说"最近的聊天"可能指：
- 最近一次（最常见）
- 最近几次（"我这两天的讨论"）
- 最近关于某个主题的（"上次讨论部署的那次"）

第一版可以只处理"最近一次、本项目"，后续扩展。

### 5. 触发方式

两种可能：
- 被动触发：用户明确说"看看最近的聊天"
- 主动触发：Skill 激活时如果检测到有未同步的新 transcript，主动提示"发现你上次在这个项目有一个未完成的会话，要看看吗？"

主动触发体验更好，但需要在 On Activation 流程中增加检测步骤。

---

## 总结

你的需求本质是 跨会话上下文恢复——这是 MemoryTree 最核心的价值场景之一。它需要：

1. 一个即时同步路径（不依赖心跳定时）
2. 一个项目级 transcript 定位能力（找到本项目最新的会话）
3. 一个智能总结步骤（模型读取 transcript，提取延续所需的上下文）
4. 可能的 On Activation 主动检测（不等用户问，主动发现未同步的会话）

你看我理解的对不对？有哪些点需要调整或补充？

user #278 02:58:54

assistant #279 02:59:47

对，这两点很重要。展开讨论一下： --- ## 1. 心跳默认 5 分钟 + 激活时询问 回到 TODO.md 最初的设计（300s）是合理的——如果核心价值场景是"跨会话上下文恢复"，5 分钟意味着用户关掉窗口后最多等 5 分钟 transcript 就会被同步。15 分钟太长了，用户很可能在这个间隔内就开了新会话。 **激活时询问的时机**：当 Skill 激活检测到 daemon 未注册时，不是简单提示 `memorytre...

Expand full message

对，这两点很重要。展开讨论一下：

---

## 1. 心跳默认 5 分钟 + 激活时询问

回到 TODO.md 最初的设计（300s）是合理的——如果核心价值场景是"跨会话上下文恢复"，5 分钟意味着用户关掉窗口后最多等 5 分钟 transcript 就会被同步。15 分钟太长了，用户很可能在这个间隔内就开了新会话。

激活时询问的时机：当 Skill 激活检测到 daemon 未注册时，不是简单提示 memorytree-daemon install，而是交互式地问：

检测到心跳尚未注册。是否启用自动 transcript 同步？
  - 间隔：默认 5 分钟（可自定义）
  - 范围：当前项目 + 已注册的其他项目
  - 自动推送：是/否

用户确认后再执行 install。这比"静默建议一个命令让用户自己跑"体验好得多。

同时 config.toml 里的 heartbeat_interval 默认值从 "15m" 改为 "5m"。

---

## 2. 跨客户端覆盖：Claude + Codex + Gemini

这才是这个功能真正强大的地方。场景不只是：

> Claude 会话 A → Claude 会话 B（同工具切换）

而是：

> 任何 AI 助手的会话 → 任何 AI 助手的新会话

举几个真实场景：

| 上一次 | 这一次 | 用户说 |
|--------|--------|--------|
| Codex 长会话讨论架构 | 开 Claude Code | "看看我最近的聊天" |
| Gemini CLI 调试问题 | 开 Claude Code | "上次调试到哪了" |
| Claude Code 会话 A | Claude Code 会话 B | "继续上次的问题" |
| Codex 会话 | Codex 新会话 | "最近讨论了什么" |

这意味着即时同步的扫描范围不是只看一个客户端，而是三个客户端的 transcript 存储全部扫描，然后按以下维度筛选：

1. 项目归属：transcript 的 cwd/repo path 匹配当前项目
2. 时间排序：跨客户端统一排序，找到最新的
3. 排除当前会话：不要把刚开始的会话 B 当成"最近的聊天"

最终输出的总结需要标注来源：

你最近一次关于此项目的会话：
- 客户端：Codex
- 时间：2026-03-15 14:30
- 时长：约 2 小时
- 主题：重构认证模块
- 进展：完成了 JWT 替换，但 refresh token 逻辑有 bug 未解决
- 卡点：TokenService.rotate() 在并发场景下会丢失 session...

---

## 这引出一个架构问题

当前的 transcript 系统有两层：

| 层 | 位置 | 职责 |
|----|------|------|
| 项目镜像 | Memory/06_transcripts/ | 只存本项目的 transcript |
| 全局归档 | ~/.memorytree/transcripts/ | 存所有项目、所有客户端 |

"即时同步 + 定位最新会话"这个操作的查询入口应该是全局归档的索引（sessions.jsonl / search.sqlite），因为：

- 需要跨客户端查询
- 需要按项目路径过滤
- 需要按时间排序
- 全局索引是唯一能满足这些维度的地方

流程变成：

用户说"看看最近的聊天"
  → 即时同步（扫描三个客户端目录，增量导入到全局归档 + 项目镜像）
  → 查询全局索引：WHERE project = 当前目录 ORDER BY timestamp DESC LIMIT 1
  → 排除当前会话 ID
  → 读取 clean transcript（或 raw 如果 clean 还没生成）
  → 模型生成"延续摘要"
  → 输出给用户

---

## 总结这次讨论的两个决策

1. heartbeat_interval 默认改为 "5m"，Skill 激活时若 daemon 未注册则交互式询问用户配置偏好后再 install
2. "查看最近聊天"功能覆盖全部三个客户端（Claude Code、Codex、Gemini CLI），通过全局归档索引做跨客户端、按项目筛选的统一查询

这两个决策要不要先记到 TODO.md 或者某个设计文档里，等后续一起落地？

user #280 03:03:13

对，讨论非常有价值，我们先记录到todo，还有就是用的询问 需要适配所有支持skill的大模型 对吧 比如claude code codex gemini

Show output payload 
1→# memorytree-workflow Skill 开发待办
     2→
     3→## 背景
     4→
     5→本仓库是 MemoryTree 记忆系统的 Skill 实现，从 `beyondchenlin/memorytree` 独立拆出。
     6→消费端项目（普通项目）通过安装此 Skill 获得记忆管理能力。
     7→
     8→## 当前核心问题
     9→
    10→大模型不可靠 — 会忘记执行 chat_log 记录、transcript 导入等机械性流程。
    11→需要把这些确定性工作交给后台进程，大模型只做轻量的摘要和状态更新。
    12→
    13→## 架构决策
    14→
    15→### 定时心跳（单次执行 + OS 调度，零 token 消耗）
    16→- 全局单实例: 1 台电脑 = 1 个定时任务，扫描所有项目
    17→- 定时扫描客户端 transcript 目录（~/.claude/projects/、~/.codex/sessions/、~/.gemini/）
    18→- 每次执行: 扫描 → 增量导入 → 清洗 → git commit + push → 退出
    19→- 心跳间隔可配置（默认 5 分钟）
    20→- 运行方式: OS 原生定时任务（cron / launchd / Task Scheduler），tmux 仅用于开发调试
    21→- Git 策略: **默认 auto_push = true**（记忆数据安全优先，防止本地丢失）
    22→
    23→### 大模型（对话时才介入，省 token）
    24→- 只写当轮 chat_log 摘要（几行文字）
    25→- 只更新 goal/todo 状态
    26→- 不负责 transcript 导入、清洗、提交
    27→
    28→---
    29→
    30→## 待办任务（按优先级排序）
    31→
    32→### P0: 定时心跳 — 核心阻塞项
    33→
    34→> 验收标准: 全局单实例 heartbeat，OS 定时任务驱动，自动发现新 transcript 并完成导入 + commit + push，无需人工介入。
    35→
    36→- [ ] **运行架构**: 单次执行 + OS 原生调度
    37→  - 核心脚本 `heartbeat.py`: 跑一次扫描就退出（无状态、幂等）
    38→  - OS 定时任务每 5 分钟调用一次
    39→  - `memorytree-daemon install`: 自动检测 OS，注册定时任务
    40→  - `memorytree-daemon run-once`: 手动执行一次（调试用）
    41→  - `memorytree-daemon watch`: tmux 循环模式（开发调试用）
    42→  - `memorytree-daemon uninstall`: 移除定时任务
    43→- [ ] **首次安装触发**: `init-memorytree.py` 完成后提示用户运行 `memorytree-daemon install`（不静默注册，需用户确认）
    44→- [ ] **实现 heartbeat 主逻辑**: 调用 `discover-transcripts.py` 扫描三个客户端目录
    45→- [ ] **增量导入**: 发现新 transcript 时调用 `import-transcripts.py`
    46→- [ ] **敏感信息预检**: 清洗阶段扫描常见敏感模式（API key、password、token），发现时标记警告写入日志（不自动删除，避免误伤）
    47→- [ ] **自动 git 提交 + 推送**:
    48→  - `git status --porcelain Memory/` → 无变动则跳过
    49→  - 有变动 → `git add Memory/` → `git commit` → `git push`
    50→  - **默认 auto_push = true**（记忆是用户资产，本地丢失不可恢复）
    51→  - 首次安装时提示用户确认 push 行为，之后静默执行
    52→  - 提交到当前分支（遵循项目已有的分支策略，不自动创建新分支）
    53→- [ ] **git remote 前置检查**:
    54→  - push 前检测 remote 是否存在
    55→  - 无 remote → 跳过 push，记录到日志，下次 Skill 激活时提醒用户配置
    56→- [ ] **配置文件** (`~/.memorytree/config.toml`，全局唯一):
    57→  - 心跳间隔（默认 300s）
    58→  - 监听目录列表（默认三个客户端目录）
    59→  - 已注册项目列表（自动发现或手动添加）
    60→  - auto_push 开关（默认 true）
    61→  - log_level（默认 info）
    62→- [ ] **运维能力**:
    63→  - 日志输出到 `~/.memorytree/logs/heartbeat.log`（按日轮转）
    64→  - 锁文件互斥（防止上一次还没跑完，下一次又启动）
    65→  - push 失败时记录到日志，下次重试（网络中断不丢数据）
    66→  - **错误通知**: 连续失败 N 次后，写入 `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`，下次 Skill 激活时展示给用户
    67→- [ ] **测试用例**:
    68→  - 单元测试: 配置加载、增量检测逻辑、锁文件互斥
    69→  - 集成测试: 模拟新 transcript 出现 → 验证自动导入 + commit
    70→
    71→### P1: 代码质量 — 降低维护成本
    72→
    73→> 验收标准: `_transcript_utils.py` 拆分为 ≤400 行的模块；CI 流水线自动运行测试。
    74→
    75→- [ ] **拆分 `_transcript_utils.py`**（当前 927 行，职责过重）:
    76→  - `_transcript_parse.py` — 三客户端解析逻辑（Codex/Claude/Gemini）
    77→  - `_transcript_import.py` — 导入、清洗、manifest 生成
    78→  - `_transcript_index.py` — SQLite 索引操作
    79→  - `_transcript_discover.py` — 文件发现、项目匹配
    80→- [ ] **transcript 去重修复**:
    81→  - 基于 `timestamp + role + content_hash` 三元组去重
    82→  - 补充回归测试（构造含重复消息的 fixture）
    83→- [ ] **CI 流水线** (GitHub Actions):
    84→  - Python 3.10+ 矩阵测试（Linux/macOS/Windows）
    85→  - 测试覆盖率报告（目标 ≥ 80%）
    86→- [ ] **集成测试补充**:
    87→  - init → upgrade → import 端到端流程
    88→  - discover 批量扫描流程
    89→
    90→### P2: 大模型职责最小化 — 依赖 P0 完成
    91→
    92→> 验收标准: SKILL.md 明确标注哪些工作由 heartbeat 负责；transcript 处理段落标记为"大模型不执行"。
    93→
    94→- [ ] **更新 SKILL.md On Activation 段**:
    95→  - 明确大模型只负责: chat_log 摘要 + goal/todo 更新
    96→  - 明确 heartbeat 负责: transcript 发现/导入/清洗/提交
    97→- [ ] **添加定时任务状态检测**:
    98→  - On Activation 时检测定时任务是否已注册（检查 crontab / launchd plist / schtasks）
    99→  - 未注册则提示用户运行 `memorytree-daemon install`
   100→  - 读取 `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`，展示累积的错误通知
   101→
   102→### P3: 分发与兼容 — 降低使用门槛
   103→
   104→> 验收标准: 用户可通过 `pip install` 或 `uv add` 安装；跨平台路径正确。
   105→
   106→- [ ] **项目包化**:
   107→  - 添加 `pyproject.toml`（含版本号、入口点、依赖声明）
   108→  - CLI 入口: `memorytree-daemon install|uninstall|run-once|watch|status`
   109→- [ ] **跨平台路径兼容**:
   110→  - Windows: `%APPDATA%` / `%LOCALAPPDATA%` 路径适配
   111→  - 测试矩阵覆盖 Windows/macOS/Linux
   112→- [ ] **安装文档更新**:
   113→  - pip install 方式
   114→  - Claude Code skill 安装方式
   115→  - 各平台定时任务注册说明（自动 install 失败时的手动方案）
   116→
   117→---
   118→
   119→## 任务依赖关系
   120→
   121→```
   122→P1 (代码拆分) ─先做→ P0 (heartbeat 复用拆分后的模块)
   123→P0 (heartbeat) ─先做→ P2 (职责最小化，需要 heartbeat 就绪)
   124→P0 (heartbeat) ─先做→ P3 (打包需包含 heartbeat)
   125→```
   126→
   127→建议执行顺序: **P1 拆分 → P0 heartbeat → P2 职责更新 → P3 分发**
   128→
   129→---
   130→
   131→## 相关仓库
   132→
   133→- 消费端项目: `beyondchenlin/memorytree`（目标 v002 已创建）
   134→- 本仓库: `beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow`
   135→
   136→## 现有脚本（可复用）
   137→
   138→| 脚本 | 用途 |
   139→|------|------|
   140→| init-memorytree.py | 新项目初始化记忆目录结构 |
   141→| upgrade-memorytree.py | 安全升级已有项目 |
   142→| import-transcripts.py | 单文件 transcript 导入（raw → clean → manifest → SQLite） |
   143→| discover-transcripts.py | 批量扫描三个客户端的 transcript |
   144→| detect-memorytree-locale.py | 检测项目语言环境 |
   145→
   146→## 远期愿景: OpenMnemo
   147→
   148→以 Gitea 为底座的 SaaS 记忆平台，跨平台 AI 对话聚合 + 语义检索 + Git 版本管理。
   149→项目名 OpenMnemo（来自希腊记忆女神 Mnemosyne），域名和 GitHub org 全线可用。
   150→
   151→预研课题（待独立为单独文档）:
   152→- [ ] Gitea API 批量写入验证（POST /api/v1/repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents）
   153→- [ ] 多租户仓库结构设计（每用户一个 Git 仓库）
   154→- [ ] 语义检索方案: BM25 (SQLite FTS5) + 向量搜索
   155→- [ ] 浏览器插件可行性: 从 ChatGPT/Claude/Gemini 网页版采集对话
   156→
