  "old_string": "### P0: 定时心跳 — 核心阻塞项\n\n> 验收标准: 全局单实例 heartbeat，OS 定时任务驱动，自动发现新 transcript 并完成导入 + commit + push，无需人工介入。\n\n- [ ] **运行架构**: 单次执行 + OS 原生调度\n  - 核心脚本 `heartbeat.py`: 跑一次扫描就退出（无状态、幂等）\n  - OS 定时任务每 5 分钟调用一次\n  - `memorytree-daemon install`: 自动检测 OS，注册定时任务\n  - `memorytree-daemon run-once`: 手动执行一次（调试用）\n  - `memorytree-daemon watch`: tmux 循环模式（开发调试用）\n  - `memorytree-daemon uninstall`: 移除定时任务\n- [ ] **首次安装触发**: `init-memorytree.py` 完成后提示用户运行 `memorytree-daemon install`（不静默注册，需用户确认）\n- [ ] **实现 heartbeat 主逻辑**: 调用 `discover-transcripts.py` 扫描三个客户端目录\n- [ ] **增量导入**: 发现新 transcript 时调用 `import-transcripts.py`\n- [ ] **敏感信息预检**: 清洗阶段扫描常见敏感模式（API key、password、token），发现时标记警告写入日志（不自动删除，避免误伤）\n- [ ] **自动 git 提交 + 推送**:\n  - `git status --porcelain Memory/` → 无变动则跳过\n  - 有变动 → `git add Memory/` → `git commit` → `git push`\n  - **默认 auto_push = true**（记忆是用户资产，本地丢失不可恢复）\n  - 首次安装时提示用户确认 push 行为，之后静默执行\n  - 提交到当前分支（遵循项目已有的分支策略，不自动创建新分支）\n- [ ] **git remote 前置检查**:\n  - push 前检测 remote 是否存在\n  - 无 remote → 跳过 push，记录到日志，下次 Skill 激活时提醒用户配置\n- [ ] **配置文件** (`~/.memorytree/config.toml`，全局唯一):\n  - 心跳间隔（默认 300s）\n  - 监听目录列表（默认三个客户端目录）\n  - 已注册项目列表（自动发现或手动添加）\n  - auto_push 开关（默认 true）\n  - log_level（默认 info）\n- [ ] **运维能力**:\n  - 日志输出到 `~/.memorytree/logs/heartbeat.log`（按日轮转）\n  - 锁文件互斥（防止上一次还没跑完，下一次又启动）\n  - push 失败时记录到日志，下次重试（网络中断不丢数据）\n  - **错误通知**: 连续失败 N 次后，写入 `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`，下次 Skill 激活时展示给用户\n- [ ] **测试用例**:\n  - 单元测试: 配置加载、增量检测逻辑、锁文件互斥\n  - 集成测试: 模拟新 transcript 出现 → 验证自动导入 + commit",
  "new_string": "### P0: 定时心跳 — 核心阻塞项\n\n> 验收标准: 全局单实例 heartbeat，OS 定时任务驱动，自动发现新 transcript 并完成导入 + commit + push，无需人工介入。\n\n- [ ] **运行架构**: 单次执行 + OS 原生调度\n  - 核心脚本 `heartbeat.py`: 跑一次扫描就退出（无状态、幂等）\n  - OS 定时任务每 5 分钟调用一次\n  - `memorytree-daemon install`: 自动检测 OS，注册定时任务\n  - `memorytree-daemon run-once`: 手动执行一次（调试用）\n  - `memorytree-daemon watch`: tmux 循环模式（开发调试用）\n  - `memorytree-daemon uninstall`: 移除定时任务\n- [ ] **首次安装触发**: Skill 激活时若 daemon 未注册，交互式询问用户配置偏好（间隔、auto_push、范围），确认后执行 install。询问方式必须兼容所有支持 Skill 的大模型（Claude Code、Codex、Gemini CLI），不依赖特定客户端的 UI 组件\n- [ ] **实现 heartbeat 主逻辑**: 调用 `discover-transcripts.py` 扫描三个客户端目录\n- [ ] **增量导入**: 发现新 transcript 时调用 `import-transcripts.py`\n- [ ] **敏感信息预检**: 清洗阶段扫描常见敏感模式（API key、password、token），发现时标记警告写入日志（不自动删除，避免误伤）\n- [ ] **自动 git 提交 + 推送**:\n  - `git status --porcelain Memory/` → 无变动则跳过\n  - 有变动 → `git add Memory/` → `git commit` → `git push`\n  - **默认 auto_push = true**（记忆是用户资产，本地丢失不可恢复）\n  - 首次安装时提示用户确认 push 行为，之后静默执行\n  - 提交到当前分支（遵循项目已有的分支策略，不自动创建新分支）\n- [ ] **git remote 前置检查**:\n  - push 前检测 remote 是否存在\n  - 无 remote → 跳过 push，记录到日志，下次 Skill 激活时提醒用户配置\n- [ ] **配置文件** (`~/.memorytree/config.toml`，全局唯一):\n  - 心跳间隔（默认 300s）\n  - 监听目录列表（默认三个客户端目录）\n  - 已注册项目列表（自动发现或手动添加）\n  - auto_push 开关（默认 true）\n  - log_level（默认 info）\n- [ ] **运维能力**:\n  - 日志输出到 `~/.memorytree/logs/heartbeat.log`（按日轮转）\n  - 锁文件互斥（防止上一次还没跑完，下一次又启动）\n  - push 失败时记录到日志，下次重试（网络中断不丢数据）\n  - **错误通知**: 连续失败 N 次后，写入 `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`，下次 Skill 激活时展示给用户\n- [ ] **测试用例**:\n  - 单元测试: 配置加载、增量检测逻辑、锁文件互斥\n  - 集成测试: 模拟新 transcript 出现 → 验证自动导入 + commit\n\n### P0.5: 跨会话上下文恢复 — 核心用户价值\n\n> 验收标准: 用户在新会话中说\"看看最近的聊天\"，Skill 即时同步最新 transcript，定位本项目最近一次会话，输出延续摘要，用户可无缝继续上次未完成的工作。\n\n**场景**: 用户在会话 A 中讨论了很长时间但问题未解决，关闭后开新会话 B，不想重新描述所有背景。\n\n**覆盖范围**: 三个客户端（Claude Code、Codex、Gemini CLI），支持跨客户端上下文恢复。例如 Codex 会话 → Claude Code 新会话。\n\n- [ ] **即时同步路径**（不依赖心跳定时）:\n  - 用户请求时立即扫描三个客户端的 transcript 目录\n  - 增量导入到全局归档（`~/.memorytree/transcripts/`）和项目镜像（`Memory/06_transcripts/`）\n  - 轻量级单项目同步，不需要全量扫描所有已注册项目\n- [ ] **定位最近会话**:\n  - 查询全局索引（`sessions.jsonl` / `search.sqlite`）\n  - 过滤条件: `project = 当前目录` + `client IN (claude, codex, gemini)`\n  - 按 timestamp 降序排列\n  - 排除当前会话自身（避免把刚开始的会话 B 当成\"最近的聊天\"）\n- [ ] **延续摘要生成**（消耗 model token，但价值远大于用户手动重述）:\n  - 读取定位到的 transcript（优先 clean，精确确认查 raw）\n  - 结合 `Memory/03_chat_logs/` 已有的会话日志补充上下文\n  - 提取:\n    - 在讨论什么问题？\n    - 尝试了哪些方案？\n    - 走到了哪一步？\n    - 什么没有解决？最后卡在哪里？\n  - 输出来源标注（客户端、时间、时长）\n- [ ] **On Activation 主动检测**（可选增强）:\n  - Skill 激活时自动检测是否有未同步的新 transcript 属于当前项目\n  - 有则主动提示: \"发现你上次在这个项目有一个未完成的会话（Codex, 2h ago），要查看摘要吗？\"\n  - 不等用户主动询问，降低使用门槛\n- [ ] **扩展查询能力**（后续迭代）:\n  - \"最近几次的聊天\"（不限于一次）\n  - \"上次讨论部署的那次\"（按主题查找）\n  - 跨项目查找: \"我在其他项目里讨论过这个问题吗？\"\n\n### 跨客户端兼容性原则\n\n> 此原则适用于所有 P0–P3 任务中涉及交互提示的部分。\n\nMemoryTree Skill 可被 Claude Code、Codex、Gemini CLI 等多个 AI 助手加载。所有交互行为必须:\n\n1. **不依赖特定客户端的 UI 组件**: 不使用 Claude Code 独有的 AskUserQuestion 多选框等控件，只使用纯文本对话\n2. **提示语言跟随用户/repo locale**: 不硬编码中英文\n3. **用自然语言提问 + 等待用户回复**: 所有确认步骤都是\"提问 → 等待回答 → 行动\"，不依赖 button/checkbox\n4. **AGENTS.md 模板中的规则必须是客户端无关的**: 不引用 Claude Code 专有概念（如 MCP、Skill tool 等）\n5. **scripts 的 CLI 接口保持统一**: 无论被哪个客户端调用，参数和输出格式一致"
}

Tool Result #313 Read toolu_01WHSKo8deH3UAeRqaHAaMZ8 03:06:36
Read output=30→7. When locale is not explicit, choose it from repo language first, then system language. Use `references/locale-selection.md`. 31→8. Keep reply language aligned with user la...
Show output payload 
30→7. When locale is not explicit, choose it from repo language first, then system language. Use `references/locale-selection.md`.
    31→8. Keep reply language aligned with user language first, then repo locale. Use `references/response-language.md`.
    32→9. For partial repos, use the non-destructive upgrade path before manual maintenance. Use `references/upgrade-path.md`.
    33→10. When transcript archival is enabled, import transcripts with deterministic code, preserve raw transcripts as evidence in both the current repo mirror and the global archive, generate cleaned Markdown for indexing, keep cross-project backfills and ambiguous ownership cases in the global archive only, and ask once per repository whether raw transcript files may be committed or pushed. Use `references/transcript-archive.md`.
    34→11. When scripts are needed, prefer an existing `uv run python` environment first, then a system `python`, and only fall back to manual scaffolding or conservative manual review if no runner is available. Use `references/execution-environment.md`.
    35→12. Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.
    36→13. Global configuration is stored in `~/.memorytree/config.toml`. See `references/global-configuration.md`.
    37→
    38→## Read Order
    39→
    40→1. Repo `AGENTS.md`, if present.
    41→2. Latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.
    42→3. Latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.
    43→4. `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.
    44→5. `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/`, transcript manifests, and repo-local raw transcript mirrors only when transcript history or cross-project memory search is relevant.
Tool Result #314 Read toolu_0181f8Lwf1XJATWwkUkgnXab 03:06:36
Read output=1→# Heartbeat Scheduling 2→ 3→Use this file when MemoryTree needs to automate transcript import, cleaning, and push without human intervention or model tokens. 4→ 5→> **Contract...
Show output payload 
1→# Heartbeat Scheduling
     2→
     3→Use this file when MemoryTree needs to automate transcript import, cleaning, and push without human intervention or model tokens.
     4→
     5→> **Contract**: `heartbeat.py` and `memorytree-daemon` are specifications for code that does not yet exist. The rules below define the intended behavior.
     6→
     7→## Goal
     8→
     9→Complete transcript discovery, import, cleaning, commit, and push without consuming model tokens or requiring human intervention.
    10→
    11→## Core Rules
    12→
    13→1. `heartbeat.py` executes once and exits. It is stateless and idempotent — safe to re-run at any interval.
    14→2. The OS-native scheduler invokes `heartbeat.py` on a fixed interval. Use cron (Linux), launchd (macOS), or Task Scheduler (Windows).
    15→3. One computer runs exactly one scheduled heartbeat task. Multiple registrations on the same machine are not supported.
    16→4. Each execution scans all three supported client directories (Codex, Claude Code, Gemini CLI).
    17→5. Each execution follows the sequence: scan → incremental import → clean → commit + push → exit.
    18→6. The `memorytree-daemon watch` mode (continuous loop) exists only for development and debugging. Production use must rely on the OS scheduler.
    19→
    20→## Daemon CLI
    21→
    22→The `memorytree-daemon` command manages the heartbeat lifecycle:
    23→
    24→| Subcommand  | Description |
    25→|-------------|-------------|
    26→| `install`   | Register the heartbeat task with the OS scheduler at the configured interval. Refuses to overwrite an existing registration. |
    27→| `uninstall` | Remove the heartbeat task from the OS scheduler. |
    28→| `run-once`  | Execute a single heartbeat cycle immediately, then exit. |
    29→| `watch`     | Run a continuous loop for development and debugging only. |
    30→| `status`    | Show whether a heartbeat task is registered and its last execution result. |
    31→
    32→## Execution Flow
    33→
    34→1. Acquire `~/.memorytree/heartbeat.lock`. If the lock is held, exit immediately.
    35→2. Load `~/.memorytree/config.toml`.
    36→3. Iterate over registered projects in `config.toml`.
    37→4. For each project: discover new transcripts → import → clean → commit.
    38→5. If `auto_push = true` and a Git remote exists, push.
    39→6. Release the lock and exit.
    40→
    41→## Sensitive Info Scanning
    42→
    43→During the cleaning step, scan each transcript for patterns that match API keys, passwords, tokens, and other secrets. When a match is found:
    44→
    45→- Log a warning with the file path and match location.
    46→- Write an alert to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.
    47→- Do **not** automatically delete or redact the content.
    48→
    49→The user reviews alerts and decides how to handle sensitive content.
    50→
    51→## Git Remote Pre-Check
    52→
    53→Before pushing, verify that the target repository has a configured Git remote:
    54→
    55→- If a remote exists, push the current branch.
    56→- If no remote is configured, skip the push, log a warning, and write an alert to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.
    57→
    58→## First Install
    59→
    60→After `scripts/init-memorytree.py` completes for the first time, prompt the user to register the heartbeat:
    61→
    62→```text
    63→Run `memorytree-daemon install` to enable automatic transcript sync.
    64→```
    65→
    66→Do not register the heartbeat task silently during initialization.
    67→
    68→## Stop And Ask
    69→
    70→Stop and ask the user before proceeding when any of these are true:
    71→
    72→1. The `install` subcommand would register a new OS-level scheduled task. Confirm the interval and scope first.
    73→2. A push target has no configured Git remote.
    74→3. An existing heartbeat registration would be overwritten by a new `install`.
    75→
Tool Result #315 Read toolu_01MVcMgFbysArpRBSnr6aX2C 03:06:36
Read output=185→``` 186→Prefer uv run python → fall back to system python → fall back to direct template scaffolding 187→``` 188→ 189→Never auto-creates `.venv` or `pyproject.toml` inside t...
Show output payload 
185→```
   186→Prefer uv run python → fall back to system python → fall back to direct template scaffolding
   187→```
   188→
   189→Never auto-creates `.venv` or `pyproject.toml` inside the project.
   190→
   191→## Background Heartbeat
   192→
   193→> **Note**: `heartbeat.py` and `memorytree-daemon` are contract specifications — code is not yet implemented.
   194→
   195→The heartbeat automates transcript discovery, import, cleaning, commit, and push without consuming model tokens or requiring human intervention.
   196→
   197→**Execution flow**:
   198→
   199→```
   200→Acquire lock → Load config.toml → Iterate projects → Discover → Import
   201→→ Clean → Commit → (auto_push) Push → Release lock → Exit
   202→```
   203→
   204→### Install the daemon
   205→
   206→```bash
   207→memorytree-daemon install
   208→```
   209→
   210→### CLI commands
   211→
   212→| Command                     | Description |
   213→|-----------------------------|-------------|
   214→| `memorytree-daemon install`   | Register the heartbeat with the OS scheduler (cron / launchd / Task Scheduler). |
   215→| `memorytree-daemon uninstall` | Remove the scheduled heartbeat task. |
   216→| `memorytree-daemon run-once`  | Run a single heartbeat cycle immediately. |
   217→| `memorytree-daemon watch`     | Continuous loop for development and debugging only. |
   218→| `memorytree-daemon status`    | Show registration state and last execution result. |
   219→
   220→### Key settings (`~/.memorytree/config.toml`)
   221→
   222→| Setting              | Default  | Description |
   223→|----------------------|----------|-------------|
   224→| `auto_push`          | `true`   | Push to remote after each commit. Skips if no remote is configured. |
   225→| `heartbeat_interval` | `"5m"`   | Interval between heartbeat executions. |
   226→| `log_level`          | `"info"` | Log verbosity (`debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error`). |
   227→
   228→### Design constraints
   229→
   230→- Single execution, stateless, idempotent — safe to re-run at any interval
   231→- One computer = one scheduled heartbeat task
   232→- Will not silently register — always confirms before `install`
   233→
   234→For details see [`references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`](references/heartbeat-scheduling.md) and [`references/global-configuration.md`](references/global-configuration.md).
   235→
   236→## Design Principles
   237→
   238→| Principle | How |
   239→|-----------|-----|
   240→| **Zero intrusion** | Never changes the repo's branch model, CI, or review process |
   241→| **Minimal dependencies** | Scripts target Python standard library; no third-party packages required at install time |
   242→| **Deterministic** | Transcript cleaning done by code, not model tokens |
   243→| **Single source of truth** | Each concept defined in one reference file, others cross-reference |
   244→| **Contract first** | Document the spec (heartbeat, daemon) before writing code |
   245→| **User asset** | Memory belongs to the user; auto_push on by default to prevent local data loss |
   246→
   247→## Reference Docs
   248→
   249→| File | Purpose |
   250→|------|---------|
   251→| [`references/project-detection.md`](references/project-detection.md) | Detect install state |
   252→| [`references/memory-layout.md`](references/memory-layout.md) | Folder and file naming rules |
   253→| [`references/update-rules.md`](references/update-rules.md) | When to version goals, todos, and session logs |
   254→| [`references/git-policy.md`](references/git-policy.md) | Git defaults, auto-push, and policy merge |
   255→| [`references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`](references/heartbeat-scheduling.md) | Heartbeat architecture, daemon CLI, execution flow |
   256→| [`references/global-configuration.md`](references/global-configuration.md) | `~/.memorytree/` layout, `config.toml` schema, `alerts.json` format |
   257→| [`references/transcript-archive.md`](references/transcript-archive.md) | Transcript archival, cleaning, search, and upload rules |
   258→| [`references/locale-selection.md`](references/locale-selection.md) | Locale choice rules |
   259→| [`references/response-language.md`](references/response-language.md) | Reply-language precedence |
   260→| [`references/execution-environment.md`](references/execution-environment.md) | How to run scripts without a project-local Python environment |
   261→| [`references/upgrade-path.md`](references/upgrade-path.md) | Safe adoption flow for partial repos |
   262→
   263→## Update
   264→
Tool Result #316 Read toolu_01V6dF4QzfpnTiWeMzQztLMf 03:06:36
Read output=128→8. Never treat the cleaned transcript as stronger evidence than the raw transcript. 129→ 130→## Search Rules 131→ 132→When the user asks to search all memories or all chat h...
Show output payload 
128→8. Never treat the cleaned transcript as stronger evidence than the raw transcript.
   129→
   130→## Search Rules
   131→
   132→When the user asks to search all memories or all chat history:
   133→
   134→1. Search `~/.memorytree/transcripts/index/search.sqlite` first for fast full-text lookup.
   135→2. Use `sessions.jsonl` for session-level metadata filtering (client, project, date range).
   136→3. Load the matching cleaned Markdown files for context. When exact wording matters, confirm against the raw transcript.
   137→4. Combine transcript results with the active repository's `Memory/` files when answering.
   138→5. If the search index does not exist, fall back to scanning the `clean/` directory directly.
   139→
   140→## Git Rules
   141→
   142→1. Cleaned transcript indexes may be committed as MemoryTree-owned documentation for the current repository.
   143→2. Raw transcript files stay in the current repository mirror regardless of upload approval.
   144→3. Raw transcript files may be staged, committed, and pushed only after the user explicitly approved raw transcript uploads for that repository.
   145→4. Even when raw transcript uploads are approved, keep the PR limited to the current repository's transcript mirror. Do not mix transcript files from unrelated projects.
   146→
Tool Result #317 Read toolu_01LTYgnZZN24M6epLkVKSSSC 03:06:36
Read output=1→# AGENTS.md 2→ 3→## Read Order 4→- Read this file first. 5→- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`. 6→- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`. 7→-...
Show output payload 
1→# AGENTS.md
     2→
     3→## Read Order
     4→- Read this file first.
     5→- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.
     6→- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.
     7→- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.
     8→- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.
     9→- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.
    10→
    11→## Memory Rules
    12→- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.
    13→- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.
    14→- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.
    15→- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.
    16→- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.
    17→
    18→## Transcript Rules
    19→- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.
    20→- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.
    21→- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.
    22→- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.
    23→- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.
    24→- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.
    25→
    26→## Git Rules
    27→- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.
    28→- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.
    29→- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.
    30→- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.
    31→- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.
    32→- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.
    33→
    34→## Heartbeat Rules
    35→- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.
    36→- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.
    37→- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.
    38→- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, suggest the user run `memorytree-daemon install`.
    39→
Tool Result #318 Read toolu_01A2KXfyEM7bxi8oAGmLke7A 03:06:36
Read output=1→# AGENTS.md 2→ 3→## 读取顺序 4→- 先读本文件。 5→- 读取 `Memory/01_goals/` 中最新的目标文件。 6→- 读取与该目标绑定的 `Memory/02_todos/` 最新待办文件。 7→- 仅在需要时读取 `Memory/04_knowledge/`。 8→- 仅在需要时读取 `Memory/06_tra...
Show output payload 
1→# AGENTS.md
     2→
     3→## 读取顺序
     4→- 先读本文件。
     5→- 读取 `Memory/01_goals/` 中最新的目标文件。
     6→- 读取与该目标绑定的 `Memory/02_todos/` 最新待办文件。
     7→- 仅在需要时读取 `Memory/04_knowledge/`。
     8→- 仅在需要时读取 `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` 和 transcript manifest。
     9→- 仅在需要时读取 `Memory/03_chat_logs/` 和 `Memory/05_archive/`。
    10→
    11→## 记忆规则
    12→- 只有在用户明确确认需求或范围变化后，才修改当前目标。
    13→- 当前待办必须与当前目标保持同步。
    14→- 对话日志只追加，禁止重写或删除既有内容。
    15→- 活跃文件保持精简，过期内容移入知识库或归档。
    16→- 清洗后的 transcript 仅用于检索和定位；当精确表述重要时，必须回看原始 transcript。
    17→
    18→## Transcript 规则
    19→- 如启用 transcript 归档，按 client、project、session 分层保存。
    20→- 原始 transcript 同时保存在当前仓库的 `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` 镜像和用户级 MemoryTree 全局归档中。
    21→- 当前仓库的 transcript 镜像只保存属于本项目的记录；其他项目的记录只进入全局 MemoryTree 归档。
    22→- 清洗后的 transcript 索引必须通过确定性代码生成，不能依赖模型重写。
    23→- 当前仓库的原始 transcript 上传权限：未设置。首次提交或推送原始 transcript 前必须先询问用户，并在此处记录答案。
    24→- 若用户未批准原始 transcript 上传，则 `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` 保留在仓库中但不参与自动暂存；当前仓库只提交清洗后的 transcript 索引或 manifest。
    25→
    26→## Git 规则
    27→- 遵守当前仓库的分支、PR、CI、评审和发布规则。
    28→- 仅自动提交和推送 MemoryTree 自有变更（`Memory/**` 以及由 MemoryTree 管理的本文件），但在当前仓库未获批前，不得包含 `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**`。
    29→- 提交标题使用 MemoryTree 专用标识。优先使用 `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`；若仓库强制其他提交规范，则使用兼容格式，例如 `docs(memorytree): <subject>`。
    30→- 对 MemoryTree 专用变更使用专用分支和 PR；仅在仓库规则允许时才开启自动合并。
    31→- 如果 diff 混入产品代码、共享策略文件、跨项目 transcript 文件或文件归属不清，暂存或推送前先询问用户。
    32→- 当 `auto_push` 启用时，心跳进程在提交后自动推送。若未配置 remote 或推送失败，会将告警写入 `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`。
    33→
    34→## 心跳规则
    35→- Transcript 的发现、导入、清洗和推送由后台心跳进程负责，大模型不执行这些操作。
    36→- 大模型在交互会话中只负责写对话日志摘要、更新目标和待办。
    37→- 如果 `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` 中有待处理的通知，在会话开始时展示给用户。
    38→- 如果 `memorytree-daemon` 可用但当前机器未注册，建议用户运行 `memorytree-daemon install`。
    39→

Show input payload 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow\\references\\heartbeat-scheduling.md",
  "old_string": "## First Install\n\nAfter `scripts/init-memorytree.py` completes for the first time, prompt the user to register the heartbeat:\n\n```text\nRun `memorytree-daemon install` to enable automatic transcript sync.\n```\n\nDo not register the heartbeat task silently during initialization.",
  "new_string": "## First Install\n\nWhen the skill activates for the first time and `memorytree-daemon` is not registered, use an interactive prompt to configure the heartbeat before installing. Ask the user for:\n\n1. Heartbeat interval (default: 5 minutes).\n2. Whether `auto_push` should be enabled (default: true).\n3. Confirmation of the project scope (which directories to scan).\n\nAfter confirmation, run `memorytree-daemon install` with the chosen settings.\n\nThe prompt must use plain-text questions and wait for the user's reply. Do not rely on client-specific UI components (buttons, checkboxes, multi-select widgets) — the same prompt must work in Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI.\n\nDo not register the heartbeat task silently during initialization.\n\n## On-Demand Sync\n\nWhen the user asks to see their most recent conversation (e.g., \"look at my last chat\"), trigger an immediate sync instead of waiting for the next scheduled heartbeat:\n\n1. Scan all three client transcript directories for the current project.\n2. Incrementally import any new transcripts into the global archive and project mirror.\n3. Query the global index (`sessions.jsonl` / `search.sqlite`) with `project = current directory`, sorted by timestamp descending.\n4. Exclude the current session to avoid returning the session that just started.\n5. Load the matched transcript and generate a continuation summary using model tokens.\n\nThe continuation summary should extract:\n\n- What problem was being discussed.\n- What approaches were tried.\n- How far the work progressed.\n- What remains unresolved and where it got stuck.\n\nThis path is distinct from the scheduled heartbeat: it is user-triggered, immediate, scoped to one project, and produces a model-generated summary. The scheduled heartbeat is background-only and never consumes model tokens.\n\nSee `references/transcript-archive.md` for the session continuity search flow."
}

  "new_string": "## Stop And Ask\n\nStop and ask the user before proceeding when any of these are true:\n\n1. The `install` subcommand would register a new OS-level scheduled task. Confirm the interval, auto_push preference, and scope first.\n2. A push target has no configured Git remote.\n3. An existing heartbeat registration would be overwritten by a new `install`.\n\n## Cross-Client Compatibility\n\nAll interactive prompts in this skill (heartbeat install, configuration, on-demand sync confirmation) must work across every AI assistant that supports the skill:\n\n1. Use plain-text questions and wait for the user's text reply. Do not use client-specific UI components.\n2. Follow the user's language or the repo locale for prompt text. Do not hardcode a single language.\n3. Keep script CLI interfaces uniform regardless of which client invokes them."
}

  "new_string": "5. If the search index does not exist, fall back to scanning the `clean/` directory directly.\n\n## Session Continuity\n\nWhen the user opens a new session and asks to see their most recent conversation, the skill provides cross-session context recovery:\n\n1. Trigger an on-demand sync: immediately scan all three client transcript directories for the current project, bypassing the scheduled heartbeat interval. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.\n2. Query the global index with `project = current directory` and `client IN (claude, codex, gemini)`, sorted by timestamp descending.\n3. Exclude the current session ID to avoid returning the session that just started.\n4. Load the located transcript: prefer cleaned Markdown for readability, confirm against raw when exact wording matters.\n5. Supplement with the latest `Memory/03_chat_logs/` entry if the previous session wrote a chat log summary.\n6. Generate a continuation summary (consumes model tokens) that extracts: the problem under discussion, approaches tried, progress made, and what remains unresolved.\n7. Include source metadata in the output: client name, session timestamp, approximate duration.\n\nThis feature covers all three clients. A user who worked in Codex and then opens Claude Code can recover the Codex session context, and vice versa.\n\n## Git Rules"
}

  "new_string": "## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components."
}

143→3. Load matching cleaned Markdown for context; confirm exact wording against raw transcripts
   144→4. Combine with the current repo's `Memory/` files
   145→5. Fall back to scanning `clean/` directory if the index does not exist
   146→
   147→### Git Safety
   148→
   149→MemoryTree follows strict isolation rules to never interfere with your repo's workflow:
   150→
   151→| Rule | Description |
   152→|------|-------------|
   153→| File isolation | Only stages/commits `Memory/**` (excluding `raw/` until approved) and `AGENTS.md` (when managed by MemoryTree) |
   154→| Branch isolation | Uses dedicated branch `memorytree/<scope>-<date>-<slug>` |
   155→| Commit format | `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>` or repo-compatible equivalent |
   156→| PR isolation | Separate PR for MemoryTree-only changes |
   157→| Auto-merge | Only when repo still enforces its review and CI checks |
   158→| Auto-push | On by default; skips if no remote; fast-forward only |
   159→| Protected branches | Never overrides branch protection or CI rules |
   160→
   161→The skill stops and asks before proceeding in 8 scenarios: mixed product code, AGENTS.md conflicts, unclear target branch, repo forbids bot pushes, CI conflicts, unknown raw transcript permission, cross-project transcripts, or protected branch refusal.
   162→

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow\\README.md",
  "old_string": "5. Fall back to scanning `clean/` directory if the index does not exist\n\n### Git Safety",
  "new_string": "5. Fall back to scanning `clean/` directory if the index does not exist\n\n### Session Continuity\n\nWhen you open a new session and want to continue where you left off, ask MemoryTree to show your most recent conversation:\n\n> \"Look at my last chat\" / \"看看我最近的聊天\" / \"Continue where I left off\"\n\nThe skill will:\n\n1. **Instant sync** — immediately scan all three client directories (not waiting for the scheduled heartbeat)\n2. **Locate** — find the latest session for the current project across Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI\n3. **Summarize** — generate a continuation summary: what was discussed, what was tried, and what remains unresolved\n4. **Handoff** — you can say \"continue\" and pick up exactly where the previous session stopped\n\nThis works **cross-client**: a Codex session can be recovered from Claude Code, and vice versa.\n\n> **Contract**: On-demand sync is a specification — code is not yet implemented.\n\nFor details see [`references/transcript-archive.md`](references/transcript-archive.md) and [`references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`](references/heartbeat-scheduling.md).\n\n### Git Safety"
}

user #349 03:11:47

assistant #350 03:12:01

没有。我们做的全部是文档和规范，没有写任何一行可执行代码。

现在的状态：

| 层面 | 状态 |
|------|------|
| 规范/合约 | 已完成 — SKILL.md、reference docs、agent templates、README 都写好了"应该怎么做" |
| 可执行代码 | 零 — heartbeat.py、memorytree-daemon CLI、on-demand sync 逻辑全部不存在 |

用户今天打 /memorytree-workflow 然后说"看看我最近的聊天"，什么也不会发生，因为：

1. heartbeat.py 不存在 → 没有后台同步
2. memorytree-daemon 不存在 → 无法 install/run-once
3. On-demand sync 路径不存在 → Skill 无法即时扫描 transcript
4. 延续摘要生成逻辑不存在 → 无法定位和总结上次会话

要让功能真正可用，需要按 TODO.md 的优先级实现代码：

P1 (拆分 _transcript_utils.py)
  → P0 (heartbeat.py + memorytree-daemon CLI)
    → P0.5 (on-demand sync + session continuity)
      → P2 (职责最小化)
        → P3 (打包分发)

要开始写代码吗？

{
  "description": "Review 1: feasibility & dependencies",
  "prompt": "You are a senior software architect reviewing an implementation plan for a Python-based CLI tool.\n\nThe project is at D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow. It's a Claude Code \"skill\" that provides persistent project memory. The team has written extensive documentation/specifications and now wants to start implementing the actual Python code.\n\n## Your task\n\nReview the implementation plan in `TODO.md` for **feasibility, dependency correctness, risk identification, and execution order**. Also read the existing scripts in `scripts/` to understand what code already exists that can be reused.\n\n## Review checklist\n\n1. **Dependency chain correctness**: TODO says `P1 (split utils) → P0 (heartbeat) → P0.5 (session continuity) → P2 → P3`. Is this the right order? Could anything be parallelized? Are there hidden dependencies?\n\n2. **P1 feasibility**: The plan says to split `_transcript_utils.py` (927 lines) into 4 modules. Read the actual file — is 4 modules the right split? Are there other files that need refactoring first? What are the risks?\n\n3. **P0 feasibility**: The heartbeat needs:\n   - `heartbeat.py` (entry point)\n   - `memorytree-daemon` CLI (install/uninstall/run-once/watch/status)\n   - OS scheduler integration (cron/launchd/Task Scheduler)\n   - Lock file, config loading, alerts.json\n   - Git commit + push automation\n   \n   Review the existing scripts to understand what's already built. What can be reused? What needs to be written from scratch? How complex is cross-platform OS scheduler integration?\n\n4. **P0.5 feasibility**: Session continuity needs on-demand sync + global index query + model-generated summary. How much of this depends on P0 infrastructure? Could any parts be built independently?\n\n5. **Existing code quality**: Read the existing Python scripts. Are they well-structured? Do they follow the patterns described in the reference docs? Any technical debt that should be addressed before building on top?\n\n6. **Risk assessment**: What are the top 3 risks for this implementation? What could go wrong?\n\n7. **Missing items**: Is there anything the plan should include but doesn't? (e.g., error handling strategy, testing approach, migration path for existing users)\n\nRead: TODO.md, all files in scripts/, and the key reference docs (heartbeat-scheduling.md, global-configuration.md, transcript-archive.md).\n\nReport findings as:\n- CRITICAL: Blocks execution, must address first\n- HIGH: Significant risk or gap\n- MEDIUM: Should address during implementation\n- LOW: Nice to have\n\nDo NOT make any edits. Research only."
}
Tool Call #353 Agent toolu_01MeowHgsD1gRcL8DwRHBwor 03:14:21
Agent input={"description":"Review 2: spec-code alignment","prompt":"You are a specification compliance reviewer. The project at D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow has written detailed referenc...
Show input payload 
{
  "description": "Review 2: spec-code alignment",
  "prompt": "You are a specification compliance reviewer. The project at D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow has written detailed reference documentation (specs/contracts) for features that don't exist yet as code. Your job is to verify the specs are **internally consistent, complete enough to implement from, and don't have contradictions or ambiguities** that would block a developer.\n\n## Your task\n\nRead all the spec documents and verify they form a coherent, implementable blueprint.\n\n## Review checklist\n\n1. **heartbeat-scheduling.md completeness**: Does it specify enough detail to implement `heartbeat.py` and `memorytree-daemon`? Check:\n   - Is the execution flow precise enough? (What happens on errors? What's the retry policy?)\n   - Is the lock mechanism specified? (File lock? Advisory? What happens on stale locks?)\n   - Is the OS scheduler integration specified per platform? (cron syntax? launchd plist structure? schtasks command?)\n   - Is the on-demand sync flow clear enough to implement?\n\n2. **global-configuration.md completeness**: Check:\n   - Is the config.toml schema complete? (What about validation rules? What happens with invalid values?)\n   - Is the alerts.json lifecycle specified? (When are alerts created? When cleared? Max size?)\n   - Is project auto-registration specified precisely? (What triggers it? What's stored? Dedup?)\n\n3. **transcript-archive.md session continuity**: Check:\n   - How does \"exclude current session\" work technically? (Session ID? PID? Timestamp threshold?)\n   - How is \"current project\" matched across clients? (Path comparison? Normalized? Symlinks?)\n   - What happens when no matching session is found?\n   - What's the token budget for the continuation summary?\n\n4. **Cross-document contradictions**: Check for conflicting statements between:\n   - heartbeat-scheduling.md vs global-configuration.md (e.g., default values, directory paths)\n   - heartbeat-scheduling.md vs git-policy.md (e.g., auto_push behavior, branch rules)\n   - transcript-archive.md vs heartbeat-scheduling.md (e.g., which file is authoritative for the on-demand sync flow?)\n   - SKILL.md vs reference docs (e.g., rule numbers, behavior descriptions)\n\n5. **Ambiguities that would block implementation**:\n   - Any \"TBD\" or vague requirements?\n   - Any places where two reasonable interpretations exist?\n   - Any spec that says \"should\" when it means \"must\"?\n\n6. **Test specifications**: Does TODO.md's testing plan cover the critical paths? What's missing?\n\nRead: TODO.md, SKILL.md, references/heartbeat-scheduling.md, references/global-configuration.md, references/transcript-archive.md, references/git-policy.md.\n\nReport findings as:\n- CRITICAL: Ambiguity or contradiction that blocks implementation\n- HIGH: Spec gap that a developer would have to guess about\n- MEDIUM: Underspecified but reasonable defaults exist\n- LOW: Nitpick\n\nDo NOT make any edits. Research only."
}

Read output=1→# memorytree-workflow Skill 开发待办 2→ 3→## 背景 4→ 5→本仓库是 MemoryTree 记忆系统的 Skill 实现，从 `beyondchenlin/memorytree` 独立拆出。 6→消费端项目（普通项目）通过安装此 Skill 获得记忆管理能力。 7→ 8→## 当前核心问题 9→ 10→大模型不...
Show output payload 
1→# memorytree-workflow Skill 开发待办
     2→
     3→## 背景
     4→
     5→本仓库是 MemoryTree 记忆系统的 Skill 实现，从 `beyondchenlin/memorytree` 独立拆出。
     6→消费端项目（普通项目）通过安装此 Skill 获得记忆管理能力。
     7→
     8→## 当前核心问题
     9→
    10→大模型不可靠 — 会忘记执行 chat_log 记录、transcript 导入等机械性流程。
    11→需要把这些确定性工作交给后台进程，大模型只做轻量的摘要和状态更新。
    12→
    13→## 架构决策
    14→
    15→### 定时心跳（单次执行 + OS 调度，零 token 消耗）
    16→- 全局单实例: 1 台电脑 = 1 个定时任务，扫描所有项目
    17→- 定时扫描客户端 transcript 目录（~/.claude/projects/、~/.codex/sessions/、~/.gemini/）
    18→- 每次执行: 扫描 → 增量导入 → 清洗 → git commit + push → 退出
    19→- 心跳间隔可配置（默认 5 分钟）
    20→- 运行方式: OS 原生定时任务（cron / launchd / Task Scheduler），tmux 仅用于开发调试
    21→- Git 策略: **默认 auto_push = true**（记忆数据安全优先，防止本地丢失）
    22→
    23→### 大模型（对话时才介入，省 token）
    24→- 只写当轮 chat_log 摘要（几行文字）
    25→- 只更新 goal/todo 状态
    26→- 不负责 transcript 导入、清洗、提交
    27→
    28→---
    29→
    30→## 待办任务（按优先级排序）
    31→
    32→### P0: 定时心跳 — 核心阻塞项
    33→
    34→> 验收标准: 全局单实例 heartbeat，OS 定时任务驱动，自动发现新 transcript 并完成导入 + commit + push，无需人工介入。
    35→
    36→- [ ] **运行架构**: 单次执行 + OS 原生调度
    37→  - 核心脚本 `heartbeat.py`: 跑一次扫描就退出（无状态、幂等）
    38→  - OS 定时任务每 5 分钟调用一次
    39→  - `memorytree-daemon install`: 自动检测 OS，注册定时任务
    40→  - `memorytree-daemon run-once`: 手动执行一次（调试用）
    41→  - `memorytree-daemon watch`: tmux 循环模式（开发调试用）
    42→  - `memorytree-daemon uninstall`: 移除定时任务
    43→- [ ] **首次安装触发**: Skill 激活时若 daemon 未注册，交互式询问用户配置偏好（间隔、auto_push、范围），确认后执行 install。询问方式必须兼容所有支持 Skill 的大模型（Claude Code、Codex、Gemini CLI），不依赖特定客户端的 UI 组件
    44→- [ ] **实现 heartbeat 主逻辑**: 调用 `discover-transcripts.py` 扫描三个客户端目录
    45→- [ ] **增量导入**: 发现新 transcript 时调用 `import-transcripts.py`
    46→- [ ] **敏感信息预检**: 清洗阶段扫描常见敏感模式（API key、password、token），发现时标记警告写入日志（不自动删除，避免误伤）
    47→- [ ] **自动 git 提交 + 推送**:
    48→  - `git status --porcelain Memory/` → 无变动则跳过
    49→  - 有变动 → `git add Memory/` → `git commit` → `git push`
    50→  - **默认 auto_push = true**（记忆是用户资产，本地丢失不可恢复）
    51→  - 首次安装时提示用户确认 push 行为，之后静默执行
    52→  - 提交到当前分支（遵循项目已有的分支策略，不自动创建新分支）
    53→- [ ] **git remote 前置检查**:
    54→  - push 前检测 remote 是否存在
    55→  - 无 remote → 跳过 push，记录到日志，下次 Skill 激活时提醒用户配置
    56→- [ ] **配置文件** (`~/.memorytree/config.toml`，全局唯一):
    57→  - 心跳间隔（默认 300s）
    58→  - 监听目录列表（默认三个客户端目录）
    59→  - 已注册项目列表（自动发现或手动添加）
    60→  - auto_push 开关（默认 true）
    61→  - log_level（默认 info）
    62→- [ ] **运维能力**:
    63→  - 日志输出到 `~/.memorytree/logs/heartbeat.log`（按日轮转）
    64→  - 锁文件互斥（防止上一次还没跑完，下一次又启动）
    65→  - push 失败时记录到日志，下次重试（网络中断不丢数据）
    66→  - **错误通知**: 连续失败 N 次后，写入 `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`，下次 Skill 激活时展示给用户
    67→- [ ] **测试用例**:
    68→  - 单元测试: 配置加载、增量检测逻辑、锁文件互斥
    69→  - 集成测试: 模拟新 transcript 出现 → 验证自动导入 + commit
    70→
    71→### P0.5: 跨会话上下文恢复 — 核心用户价值
    72→
    73→> 验收标准: 用户在新会话中说"看看最近的聊天"，Skill 即时同步最新 transcript，定位本项目最近一次会话，输出延续摘要，用户可无缝继续上次未完成的工作。
    74→
    75→**场景**: 用户在会话 A 中讨论了很长时间但问题未解决，关闭后开新会话 B，不想重新描述所有背景。
    76→
    77→**覆盖范围**: 三个客户端（Claude Code、Codex、Gemini CLI），支持跨客户端上下文恢复。例如 Codex 会话 → Claude Code 新会话。
    78→
    79→- [ ] **即时同步路径**（不依赖心跳定时）:
    80→  - 用户请求时立即扫描三个客户端的 transcript 目录
    81→  - 增量导入到全局归档（`~/.memorytree/transcripts/`）和项目镜像（`Memory/06_transcripts/`）
    82→  - 轻量级单项目同步，不需要全量扫描所有已注册项目
    83→- [ ] **定位最近会话**:
    84→  - 查询全局索引（`sessions.jsonl` / `search.sqlite`）
    85→  - 过滤条件: `project = 当前目录` + `client IN (claude, codex, gemini)`
    86→  - 按 timestamp 降序排列
    87→  - 排除当前会话自身（避免把刚开始的会话 B 当成"最近的聊天"）
    88→- [ ] **延续摘要生成**（消耗 model token，但价值远大于用户手动重述）:
    89→  - 读取定位到的 transcript（优先 clean，精确确认查 raw）
    90→  - 结合 `Memory/03_chat_logs/` 已有的会话日志补充上下文
    91→  - 提取:
    92→    - 在讨论什么问题？
    93→    - 尝试了哪些方案？
    94→    - 走到了哪一步？
    95→    - 什么没有解决？最后卡在哪里？
    96→  - 输出来源标注（客户端、时间、时长）
    97→- [ ] **On Activation 主动检测**（可选增强）:
    98→  - Skill 激活时自动检测是否有未同步的新 transcript 属于当前项目
    99→  - 有则主动提示: "发现你上次在这个项目有一个未完成的会话（Codex, 2h ago），要查看摘要吗？"
   100→  - 不等用户主动询问，降低使用门槛
   101→- [ ] **扩展查询能力**（后续迭代）:
   102→  - "最近几次的聊天"（不限于一次）
   103→  - "上次讨论部署的那次"（按主题查找）
   104→  - 跨项目查找: "我在其他项目里讨论过这个问题吗？"
   105→
   106→### 跨客户端兼容性原则
   107→
   108→> 此原则适用于所有 P0–P3 任务中涉及交互提示的部分。
   109→
   110→MemoryTree Skill 可被 Claude Code、Codex、Gemini CLI 等多个 AI 助手加载。所有交互行为必须:
   111→
   112→1. **不依赖特定客户端的 UI 组件**: 不使用 Claude Code 独有的 AskUserQuestion 多选框等控件，只使用纯文本对话
   113→2. **提示语言跟随用户/repo locale**: 不硬编码中英文
   114→3. **用自然语言提问 + 等待用户回复**: 所有确认步骤都是"提问 → 等待回答 → 行动"，不依赖 button/checkbox
   115→4. **AGENTS.md 模板中的规则必须是客户端无关的**: 不引用 Claude Code 专有概念（如 MCP、Skill tool 等）
   116→5. **scripts 的 CLI 接口保持统一**: 无论被哪个客户端调用，参数和输出格式一致
   117→
   118→### P1: 代码质量 — 降低维护成本
   119→
   120→> 验收标准: `_transcript_utils.py` 拆分为 ≤400 行的模块；CI 流水线自动运行测试。
   121→
   122→- [ ] **拆分 `_transcript_utils.py`**（当前 927 行，职责过重）:
   123→  - `_transcript_parse.py` — 三客户端解析逻辑（Codex/Claude/Gemini）
   124→  - `_transcript_import.py` — 导入、清洗、manifest 生成
   125→  - `_transcript_index.py` — SQLite 索引操作
   126→  - `_transcript_discover.py` — 文件发现、项目匹配
   127→- [ ] **transcript 去重修复**:
   128→  - 基于 `timestamp + role + content_hash` 三元组去重
   129→  - 补充回归测试（构造含重复消息的 fixture）
   130→- [ ] **CI 流水线** (GitHub Actions):
   131→  - Python 3.10+ 矩阵测试（Linux/macOS/Windows）
   132→  - 测试覆盖率报告（目标 ≥ 80%）
   133→- [ ] **集成测试补充**:
   134→  - init → upgrade → import 端到端流程
   135→  - discover 批量扫描流程
   136→
   137→### P2: 大模型职责最小化 — 依赖 P0 完成
   138→
   139→> 验收标准: SKILL.md 明确标注哪些工作由 heartbeat 负责；transcript 处理段落标记为"大模型不执行"。
   140→
   141→- [ ] **更新 SKILL.md On Activation 段**:
   142→  - 明确大模型只负责: chat_log 摘要 + goal/todo 更新
   143→  - 明确 heartbeat 负责: transcript 发现/导入/清洗/提交
   144→- [ ] **添加定时任务状态检测**:
   145→  - On Activation 时检测定时任务是否已注册（检查 crontab / launchd plist / schtasks）
   146→  - 未注册则提示用户运行 `memorytree-daemon install`
   147→  - 读取 `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`，展示累积的错误通知
   148→
   149→### P3: 分发与兼容 — 降低使用门槛
   150→
   151→> 验收标准: 用户可通过 `pip install` 或 `uv add` 安装；跨平台路径正确。
   152→
   153→- [ ] **项目包化**:
   154→  - 添加 `pyproject.toml`（含版本号、入口点、依赖声明）
   155→  - CLI 入口: `memorytree-daemon install|uninstall|run-once|watch|status`
   156→- [ ] **跨平台路径兼容**:
   157→  - Windows: `%APPDATA%` / `%LOCALAPPDATA%` 路径适配
   158→  - 测试矩阵覆盖 Windows/macOS/Linux
   159→- [ ] **安装文档更新**:
   160→  - pip install 方式
   161→  - Claude Code skill 安装方式
   162→  - 各平台定时任务注册说明（自动 install 失败时的手动方案）
   163→
   164→---
   165→
   166→## 任务依赖关系
   167→
   168→```
   169→P1 (代码拆分) ─先做→ P0 (heartbeat 复用拆分后的模块)
   170→P0 (heartbeat) ─先做→ P0.5 (上下文恢复，复用即时同步 + 全局索引)
   171→P0 (heartbeat) ─先做→ P2 (职责最小化，需要 heartbeat 就绪)
   172→P0 (heartbeat) ─先做→ P3 (打包需包含 heartbeat)
   173→跨客户端兼容性原则 ─贯穿→ 所有交互设计
   174→```
   175→
   176→建议执行顺序: **P1 拆分 → P0 heartbeat → P0.5 上下文恢复 → P2 职责更新 → P3 分发**
   177→
   178→---
   179→
   180→## 相关仓库
   181→
   182→- 消费端项目: `beyondchenlin/memorytree`（目标 v002 已创建）
   183→- 本仓库: `beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow`
   184→
   185→## 现有脚本（可复用）
   186→
   187→| 脚本 | 用途 |
   188→|------|------|
   189→| init-memorytree.py | 新项目初始化记忆目录结构 |
   190→| upgrade-memorytree.py | 安全升级已有项目 |
   191→| import-transcripts.py | 单文件 transcript 导入（raw → clean → manifest → SQLite） |
   192→| discover-transcripts.py | 批量扫描三个客户端的 transcript |
   193→| detect-memorytree-locale.py | 检测项目语言环境 |
   194→
   195→## 远期愿景: OpenMnemo
   196→
   197→以 Gitea 为底座的 SaaS 记忆平台，跨平台 AI 对话聚合 + 语义检索 + Git 版本管理。
   198→项目名 OpenMnemo（来自希腊记忆女神 Mnemosyne），域名和 GitHub org 全线可用。
   199→
   200→预研课题（待独立为单独文档）:
   201→- [ ] Gitea API 批量写入验证（POST /api/v1/repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents）
   202→- [ ] 多租户仓库结构设计（每用户一个 Git 仓库）
   203→- [ ] 语义检索方案: BM25 (SQLite FTS5) + 向量搜索
   204→- [ ] 浏览器插件可行性: 从 ChatGPT/Claude/Gemini 网页版采集对话
   205→
